10: Worktree-Backed Heartbeat  •  11: Cross-Client Recall  •  12: Managed Caddy Hosting  •  13: Static Report Publishing 14:

15: 16: [![License: MIT](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-MIT-blue.svg)](LICENSE) 17: [![Node.js 20+](https://img.shields.io/badge/Node.js-20%2B-339933.svg?logo=node.js&logoColor=white)](https://nodejs.org/) 18: [![Platform](https://img.shields.io/badge/Platform-macOS%20%7C%20Linux%20%7C%20Windows-lightgrey.svg)](#requirements) 19: 20:

21: Quick Start  •  22: Architecture  •  23: Capabilities  •  24: Heartbeat  •  25: Caddy  •  26: CLI  •  27: Docs 28:

29: 30:
31: 32: --- 33: 34: ## Overview 35: 36: MemoryTree gives AI-assisted repositories a durable memory layer that survives across sessions, clients, branches, and machines. 37: 38: This repository ships two things: 39: 40: 1. A reusable skill defined by [SKILL.md](SKILL.md) 41: 2. A Node.js CLI for workspace setup, transcript import, session recall, report generation, heartbeat automation, and managed local hosting 42: 43: The current implementation is built around three storage scopes: 44: 45: - The development repository you actively edit 46: - A dedicated MemoryTree worktree that the heartbeat can update safely 47: - A global archive under `~/.memorytree/` for cross-project transcript storage and indexing 48: 49: That model lets MemoryTree keep your main development flow clean while still importing transcripts, generating reports, and optionally publishing Memory-only updates from an isolated branch. 50: 51: ## Quick Start 52: 53: ### Install as a skill 54: 55: Claude Code: 56: 57: ```bash 58: git clone https://github.com/beyondchenlin/memorytree-workflow ~/.claude/skills/memorytree-workflow 59: cd ~/.claude/skills/memorytree-workflow 60: npm install 61: npm run build 62: ``` 63: 64: Codex: 65: 66: ```bash 67: git clone https://github.com/beyondchenlin/memorytree-workflow ~/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow 68: cd ~/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow 69: npm install 70: npm run build 71: ``` 72: 73: Windows PowerShell (Codex): 74: 75: ```powershell 76: git clone https://github.com/beyondchenlin/memorytree-workflow.git ` 77: $env:USERPROFILE\.codex\skills\memorytree-workflow 78: 79: cd $env:USERPROFILE\.codex\skills\memorytree-workflow 80: npm install 81: npm run build 82: ``` 83: 84: If your installer expects a skill path, use the repository root. `SKILL.md` lives at the top level. 85: 86: Optional: expose the CLI as `memorytree` in your shell: 87: 88: ```bash 89: npm link 90: ``` 91: 92: ### Use the skill 93: 94: In Claude Code, run: 95: 96: ```text 97: /memorytree-workflow 98: ``` 99: 100: The skill detects whether the current repository is `not-installed`, `partial`, or `installed`, then takes the next appropriate step immediately. 101: 102: ### Use the CLI directly 103: 104: `memorytree init` and `memorytree upgrade` only create or update repository files. They do not register the repository with heartbeat or modify `~/.memorytree/config.toml` by themselves. 105: 106: Recommended first-time setup for a repository that should use heartbeat: 107: 108: ```bash 109: memorytree upgrade --root . --format json 110: memorytree daemon quick-start --root . 111: memorytree caddy enable --root . 112: ``` 113: 114: Useful day-to-day commands: 115: 116: ```bash 117: memorytree recall --root . 118: memorytree discover --root . --client all --scope current-project --format json 119: memorytree report build --root . --no-ai --locale en 120: memorytree caddy status --root . 121: memorytree daemon run-once --root . --force 122: ``` 123: 124: ## Architecture 125: 126: MemoryTree now runs as a worktree-aware system, not just a single-folder transcript importer: 127: 128: ```text 129: Development repository 130: AGENTS.md 131: Memory/01_goals ... Memory/05_archive 132: | 133: | sync active context into 134: v 135: Dedicated MemoryTree worktree 136: isolated memorytree branch 137: Memory/06_transcripts 138: Memory/07_reports 139: | 140: | mirror matching transcripts, commit/push Memory-only changes, 141: | build report output 142: v 143: Global archive ~/.memorytree/ 144: config.toml 145: alerts.json 146: worktrees/ 147: transcripts/raw 148: transcripts/clean 149: transcripts/index 150: caddy/ 151: ``` 152: 153: The heartbeat copies active project context from the development directory into the dedicated worktree, processes transcript imports there, and then syncs transcript/report outputs back into the development directory. 154: 155: ### Per-repository layout 156: 157: After initialization or upgrade, a repository can contain: 158: 159: ```text 160: AGENTS.md 161: Memory/ 162: 01_goals/ 163: 02_todos/ 164: 03_chat_logs/ 165: 04_knowledge/ 166: 05_archive/ 167: 06_transcripts/ 168: clean/ 169: manifests/ 170: raw/ 171: 07_reports/ 172: ``` 173: 174: ### Global layout 175: 176: Shared state lives under `~/.memorytree/`: 177: 178: ```text 179: ~/.memorytree/ 180: config.toml 181: alerts.json 182: heartbeat.lock 183: logs/ 184: worktrees/ 185: caddy/ 186: Caddyfile 187: sites/ 188: transcripts/ 189: raw/ 190: clean/ 191: index/ 192: manifests/ 193: sessions.jsonl 194: search.sqlite 195: ``` 196: 197: ## Capabilities 198: 199: | Capability | Current behavior | 200: |---|---| 201: | Workspace scaffolding | `init` and `upgrade` create `Memory/` plus `AGENTS.md` without overwriting stronger existing repo policy | 202: | Transcript archive | `discover` scans default client stores, while `import` handles explicit transcript files and mirrors matching sessions into the repo | 203: | Session recall | `recall` runs an on-demand sync and returns the latest prior session for the current repository across clients | 204: | Worktree-backed heartbeat | `daemon register` and `daemon quick-start` create an isolated MemoryTree worktree, schedule runs, and keep context/output in sync | 205: | Report generation | `report build` creates a multi-page static site with transcripts, goals, todos, knowledge, archive, projects, graph, search, tags, and RSS | 206: | Managed local hosting | `caddy enable|disable|status` writes project-scoped Caddy fragments for long-running local report hosting | 207: | Git safety | Automatic repo-local commits stay confined to MemoryTree branches and skip raw transcript mirrors until raw uploads are approved | 208: 209: ## Transcript Sources 210: 211: Default discovery currently scans these client stores: 212: 213: | Client | Source patterns | 214: |---|---| 215: | Codex | `~/.codex/sessions/**/*.jsonl` | 216: | Claude Code | `~/.claude/projects/**/*.jsonl` | 217: | Gemini CLI | `~/.gemini/tmp/*/checkpoints/**/*.json`, `~/.gemini/tmp/*/checkpoints/**/*.jsonl`, `~/.gemini/history/**/*.json`, `~/.gemini/history/**/*.jsonl`, `~/.gemini/chats/**/*.json`, `~/.gemini/chats/**/*.jsonl` | 218: 219: Explicit one-off import also supports transcript files that are not part of default discovery, including `doubao` exports: 220: 221: ```bash 222: memorytree import --source /path/to/doubao_export.txt --client doubao --root . --force-repo 223: ``` 224: 225: Import rules: 226: 227: - Raw transcripts stay unchanged as evidence. 228: - Clean transcripts are generated deterministically by code. 229: - Repo-local mirrors are limited to transcripts that belong to the current repository unless `--force-repo` is used deliberately. 230: - Unrelated transcripts are archived in the global archive only. 231: - Raw transcript mirrors are excluded from automatic staging until the user approves raw uploads for that repository. 232: 233: ## Session Continuity 234: 235: When you ask for the most recent prior conversation, MemoryTree can: 236: 237: 1. scan the supported local transcript stores immediately 238: 2. import any new matching transcripts 239: 3. query the global index for the current project 240: 4. exclude the current session by activation time 241: 5. return the latest prior clean transcript content and metadata 242: 243: This works across clients, so a project session recorded in one assistant can be recalled from another. 244: 245: ## Report Site 246: 247: `memorytree report build` generates a static HTML site under `Memory/07_reports/`. 248: 249: Current report coverage includes: 250: 251: - dashboard 252: - transcript list and per-session pages 253: - goals, todos, knowledge, archive, and projects pages 254: - search, filters, and snippets 255: - graph view and backlink navigation 256: - tags and cached AI summaries 257: - breadcrumbs and table of contents 258: - RSS feed 259: - optional GitHub Pages deployment 260: - optional webhook notification 261: - English and Simplified Chinese locales 262: 263: Build it manually: 264: 265: ```bash 266: memorytree report build --root . --no-ai --locale en --report-base-url https://memory.example.com 267: ``` 268: 269: Temporary fallback preview: 270: 271: ```bash 272: memorytree report serve --dir ./Memory/07_reports --port 10010 273: ``` 274: 275: `report serve` is intended for temporary preview. For long-running local access, keep Caddy pointed at `Memory/07_reports/`. 276: 277: ## Heartbeat 278: 279: The heartbeat is the background automation layer. It is now project-aware and worktree-aware. 280: 281: Recommended setup: 282: 283: ```bash 284: memorytree daemon quick-start --root . 285: ``` 286: 287: Use this as the quick-install path when you want the current repository to join heartbeat with the recommended defaults. 288: 289: If you want more control, install the machine scheduler once and then register each repository with your own branch, interval, and report settings: 290: 291: ```bash 292: memorytree daemon install --interval 5m --auto-push true 293: memorytree daemon register --root . --branch memorytree-docs --heartbeat-interval 10m --refresh-interval 30m --auto-push false --generate-report true --report-port 10010 294: memorytree daemon run-once --root . --force 295: ``` 296: 297: Current execution flow: 298: 299: ```text 300: Acquire lock 301: -> load config 302: -> select due projects 303: -> ensure dedicated MemoryTree worktree 304: -> sync AGENTS.md + active Memory context into the worktree 305: -> discover and import matching transcripts 306: -> commit/push Memory-only changes on the MemoryTree branch when allowed 307: -> build Memory/07_reports when report generation is enabled and the run imported new transcripts 308: -> sync transcripts and reports back to the development directory 309: -> release lock 310: ``` 311: 312: Important branch-safety rules: 313: 314: - Repo-local transcript mirroring and automatic commits only happen on a dedicated MemoryTree branch. 315: - On non-MemoryTree branches, the heartbeat still imports into the global archive but keeps the repository clean. 316: - If no Git remote exists, push is skipped and an alert is written. 317: - Push failures alert and retry once. 318: - Sensitive transcript patterns are logged as warnings only; they are not auto-deleted or auto-redacted. 319: 320: ## Caddy 321: 322: MemoryTree includes first-stage project-managed Caddy support for local report hosting. 323: 324: Recommended commands: 325: 326: ```bash 327: memorytree caddy enable --root . 328: memorytree caddy status --root . 329: memorytree caddy disable --root . 330: ``` 331: 332: What it manages: 333: 334: - `~/.memorytree/caddy/Caddyfile` 335: - `~/.memorytree/caddy/sites/.caddy` 336: - per-project port and exposure settings taken from `~/.memorytree/config.toml` 337: 338: Exposure modes: 339: 340: - `local`: bind to `127.0.0.1` and `localhost` only 341: - `lan`: bind on the configured port and expose LAN URLs for the machine's IPv4 interfaces 342: 343: For long-running local access, `caddy enable` is the primary path. `report serve` remains the lightweight fallback. 344: 345: ## Configuration 346: 347: Global settings live in `~/.memorytree/config.toml`. 348: 349: Example: 350: 351: ```toml 352: heartbeat_interval = "5m" 353: auto_push = true 354: log_level = "info" 355: generate_report = true 356: locale = "en" 357: gh_pages_branch = "gh-pages" 358: cname = "memory.example.com" 359: webhook_url = "" 360: report_base_url = "https://memory.example.com" 361: report_port = 10010 362: report_exposure = "local" 363: 364: [[projects]] 365: id = "my-repo" 366: path = "/path/to/repo" 367: name = "my-repo" 368: development_path = "/path/to/repo" 369: memory_path = "/path/to/.memorytree/worktrees/my-repo" 370: memory_branch = "memorytree" 371: heartbeat_interval = "5m" 372: refresh_interval = "30m" 373: auto_push = true 374: generate_report = true 375: locale = "en" 376: gh_pages_branch = "gh-pages" 377: report_base_url = "https://memory.example.com" 378: report_port = 10010 379: report_exposure = "local" 380: ``` 381: 382: Fields that matter most: 383: 384: | Field | Meaning | 385: |---|---| 386: | `development_path` | The directory you actively work in | 387: | `memory_path` | The dedicated MemoryTree worktree used by the heartbeat | 388: | `memory_branch` | The branch reserved for MemoryTree automation | 389: | `heartbeat_interval` | Per-project heartbeat cadence | 390: | `refresh_interval` | How often transcript/report outputs sync back to the development directory | 391: | `generate_report` | Whether heartbeat should build `Memory/07_reports` after transcript imports | 392: | `gh_pages_branch` | Branch used for report publishing when configured | 393: | `report_base_url` | Base URL for RSS and OG metadata | 394: | `report_port` | Default local report port for this project | 395: | `report_exposure` | `local` or `lan` exposure for managed Caddy hosting | 396: 397: `path` is still preserved for compatibility, but the worktree-aware flow is driven by `development_path` and `memory_path`. 398: 399: ## Git Safety 400: 401: MemoryTree is strict about not taking over your normal development flow. 402: 403: | Rule | Behavior | 404: |---|---| 405: | Branch isolation | Automatic repo-local transcript commits happen only on dedicated MemoryTree branches | 406: | Worktree isolation | Heartbeat automation can run from a dedicated worktree instead of your main working directory | 407: | Scope isolation | Repo mirrors include only the current project's transcripts unless you force a repo-local import | 408: | Raw upload control | Raw transcript files are not auto-staged until explicitly approved | 409: | Push safety | If no remote exists, push is skipped and an alert is written | 410: | Failure handling | Push failures alert and retry once; report and webhook failures do not abort the heartbeat cycle | 411: | Secret scanning | Transcript imports log potential secrets but do not auto-delete or auto-redact them | 412: 413: ## CLI 414: 415: All commands are available through `node dist/cli.js ...`, or `memorytree ...` if you ran `npm link`. 416: 417: | Command | Description | 418: |---|---| 419: | `memorytree init` | Initialize MemoryTree files in a repository without registering heartbeat | 420: | `memorytree upgrade` | Upgrade repository files to MemoryTree without registering heartbeat | 421: | `memorytree import --source ` | Import one transcript into the repo mirror and global archive | 422: | `memorytree discover` | Scan local client stores and import matching transcripts | 423: | `memorytree locale` | Detect the effective repository locale | 424: | `memorytree recall` | Run on-demand sync and return the latest prior session | 425: | `memorytree report build` | Build the static HTML report site | 426: | `memorytree report serve` | Temporarily preview the generated report over HTTP | 427: | `memorytree caddy enable` | Write/update the current project's managed Caddy fragment and reload Caddy | 428: | `memorytree caddy disable` | Remove the current project's managed Caddy fragment and reload Caddy | 429: | `memorytree caddy status` | Show whether the current project is connected to MemoryTree-managed Caddy | 430: | `memorytree daemon install` | Register the machine-level heartbeat scheduler | 431: | `memorytree daemon quick-start` | Quick install: connect the repository to heartbeat with the recommended defaults | 432: | `memorytree daemon register` | Advanced heartbeat setup with custom per-project settings | 433: | `memorytree daemon uninstall` | Remove the scheduled heartbeat task | 434: | `memorytree daemon run-once` | Execute one heartbeat cycle immediately | 435: | `memorytree daemon watch` | Development-only continuous heartbeat loop | 436: | `memorytree daemon status` | Show scheduler registration and lock status | 437: 438: ## Development And Validation 439: 440: Install dependencies: 441: 442: ```bash 443: npm install 444: ``` 445: 446: Build: 447: 448: ```bash 449: npm run build 450: ``` 451: 452: Validate locally: 453: 454: ```bash 455: npm run typecheck 456: npm run lint 457: npm test 458: npm run test:e2e 459: ``` 460:

Configuration 346: 347: Global settings live in `~/.memorytree/config.toml`. 348: 349: Example: 350: 351: ```toml 352: heartbeat_interval = "5m" 353: auto_push = true 354: log_level = "info" 355: generate_report = true 356: locale = "en" 357: gh_pages_branch = "gh-pages" 358: cname = "memory.example.com" 359: webhook_url = "" 360: report_base_url = "https://memory.example.com" 361: report_port = 10010 362: report_exposure = "local" 363: 364: [[projects]] 365: id = "my-repo" 366: path = "/path/to/repo" 367: name = "my-repo" 368: development_path = "/path/to/repo" 369: memory_path = "/path/to/.memorytree/worktrees/my-repo" 370: memory_branch = "memorytree" 371: heartbeat_interval = "5m" 372: refresh_interval = "30m" 373: auto_push = true 374: generate_report = true 375: locale = "en" 376: gh_pages_branch = "gh-pages" 377: report_base_url = "https://memory.example.com" 378: report_port = 10010 379: report_exposure = "local" 380: ``` 381: 382: Fields that matter most: 383: 384: | Field | Meaning | 385: |---|---| 386: | `development_path` | The directory you actively work in | 387: | `memory_path` | The dedicated MemoryTree worktree used by the heartbeat | 388: | `memory_branch` | The branch reserved for MemoryTree automation | 389: | `heartbeat_interval` | Per-project heartbeat cadence | 390: | `refresh_interval` | How often transcript/report outputs sync back to the development directory | 391: | `generate_report` | Whether heartbeat should build `Memory/07_reports` after transcript imports | 392: | `gh_pages_branch` | Branch used for report publishing when configured | 393: | `report_base_url` | Base URL for RSS and OG metadata | 394: | `report_port` | Default local report port for this project | 395: | `report_exposure` | `local` or `lan` exposure for managed Caddy hosting | 396: 397: `path` is still preserved for compatibility, but the worktree-aware flow is driven by `development_path` and `memory_path`. 398: 399: ## Git Safety 400: 401: MemoryTree is strict about not taking over your normal development flow. 402: 403: | Rule | Behavior | 404: |---|---| 405: | Branch isolation | Automatic repo-local transcript commits happen only on dedicated MemoryTree branches | 406: | Worktree isolation | Heartbeat automation can run from a dedicated worktree instead of your main working directory | 407: | Scope isolation | Repo mirrors include only the current project's transcripts unless you force a repo-local import | 408: | Raw upload control | Raw transcript files are not auto-staged until explicitly approved | 409: | Push safety | If no remote exists, push is skipped and an alert is written | 410: | Failure handling | Push failures alert and retry once; report and webhook failures do not abort the heartbeat cycle | 411: | Secret scanning | Transcript imports log potential secrets but do not auto-delete or auto-redact them | 412: 413: ## CLI 414: 415: All commands are available through `node dist/cli.js ...`, or `memorytree ...` if you ran `npm link`. 416: 417: | Command | Description | 418: |---|---| 419: | `memorytree init` | Initialize MemoryTree files in a repository without registering heartbeat | 420: | `memorytree upgrade` | Upgrade repository files to MemoryTree without registering heartbeat | 421: | `memorytree import --source ` | Import one transcript into the repo mirror and global archive | 422: | `memorytree discover` | Scan local client stores and import matching transcripts | 423: | `memorytree locale` | Detect the effective repository locale | 424: | `memorytree recall` | Run on-demand sync and return the latest prior session | 425: | `memorytree report build` | Build the static HTML report site | 426: | `memorytree report serve` | Temporarily preview the generated report over HTTP | 427: | `memorytree caddy enable` | Write/update the current project's managed Caddy fragment and reload Caddy | 428: | `memorytree caddy disable` | Remove the current project's managed Caddy fragment and reload Caddy | 429: | `memorytree caddy status` | Show whether the current project is connected to MemoryTree-managed Caddy | 430: | `memorytree daemon install` | Register the machine-level heartbeat scheduler | 431: | `memorytree daemon quick-start` | Quick install: connect the repository to heartbeat with the recommended defaults | 432: | `memorytree daemon register` | Advanced heartbeat setup with custom per-project settings | 433: | `memorytree daemon uninstall` | Remove the scheduled heartbeat task | 434: | `memorytree daemon run-once` | Execute one heartbeat cycle immediately | 435: | `memorytree daemon watch` | Development-only continuous heartbeat loop | 436: | `memorytree daemon status` | Show scheduler registration and lock status | 437: 438: ## Development And Validation 439: 440: Install dependencies: 441: 442: ```bash 443: npm install 444: ``` 445: 446: Build: 447: 448: ```bash 449: npm run build 450: ``` 451: 452: Validate locally: 453: 454: ```bash 455: npm run typecheck 456: npm run lint 457: npm test 458: npm run test:e2e 459: ``` 460:

Using Node v20.20.1
Using Node v20.20.1
   1: # MemoryTree Worktree Heartbeat Upgrade Plan
   2: 
   3: ## Purpose
   4: 
   5: This document defines a worktree-based upgrade path for MemoryTree heartbeat.
   6: 
   7: The goal is to isolate automatic MemoryTree activity from the user's main development directory and main branch rules, while still making MemoryTree outputs visible inside the active development directory.
   8: 
   9: ## Problem Statement
  10: 
  11: The current behavior couples heartbeat too closely to the repository path that is registered in `~/.memorytree/config.toml`.
  12: 
  13: That creates two practical issues:
  14: 
  15: 1. If heartbeat runs in the main development directory, its automatic transcript imports and report outputs can interfere with the user's normal branch and commit flow.
  16: 2. If heartbeat does not run on a dedicated MemoryTree branch, repo-local transcript mirroring is skipped, which can leave the in-repo report looking empty even though data exists in the global archive.
  17: 
  18: ## Target Outcome
  19: 
  20: After this upgrade:
  21: 
  22: 1. Heartbeat runs only inside a dedicated Git worktree.
  23: 2. The main development directory does not run heartbeat.
  24: 3. The main development branch keeps its normal GitHub upload, review, and merge rules.
  25: 4. MemoryTree outputs generated in the worktree are copied back into the main development directory so the user can read and use them locally.
  26: 5. The system never relies on branch merges between the main development branch and the MemoryTree branch.
  27: 6. Project behavior is configured per project, while the operating system still runs a single global heartbeat task.
  28: 
  29: ## Terms
  30: 
  31: - `development directory`: the directory where the user is actively editing product code
  32: - `memory worktree`: a dedicated Git worktree used only for MemoryTree automation
  33: - `heartbeat`: the background process that discovers transcripts, imports them, and optionally builds reports
  34: - `directory sync`: copying selected files between the development directory and the memory worktree without merging branches
  35: 
  36: ## Config Scope
  37: 
  38: ### Current Limitation
  39: 
  40: The current implementation stores several behavior switches as global top-level fields in `~/.memorytree/config.toml`.
  41: 
  42: Examples:
  43: 
  44: - `heartbeat_interval`
  45: - `auto_push`
  46: - `generate_report`
  47: - `report_port`
  48: - `gh_pages_branch`
  49: - `report_base_url`
  50: 
  51: That means a quick-start action for one project can accidentally change behavior for another registered project.
  52: 
  53: ### Upgrade Direction
  54: 
  55: The target model is:
  56: 
  57: - one global scheduler
  58: - per-project execution settings
  59: 
  60: In other words:
  61: 
  62: - the operating system still runs one heartbeat task
  63: - each project entry carries its own behavior flags
  64: - the heartbeat loop decides project by project whether work is due and which options apply
  65: 
  66: ### Why A Single Global Scheduler
  67: 
  68: The upgrade should not create one OS scheduler task per project.
  69: 
  70: Reason:
  71: 
  72: - the current heartbeat runtime already assumes single-instance execution
  73: - one scheduler avoids lock contention and duplicate runs
  74: - one scheduler is simpler to operate across Windows, macOS, and Linux
  75: 
  76: Recommended model:
  77: 
  78: 1. install one global heartbeat task
  79: 2. run it on a short fixed cadence
  80: 3. evaluate each registered project independently
  81: 4. execute only the projects whose own interval and settings say they are due
  82: 
  83: ## Proposed Architecture
  84: 
  85: ### Development Directory
  86: 
  87: The development directory remains the user's normal working copy.
  88: 
  89: Responsibilities:
  90: 
  91: - product code editing
  92: - normal branch workflow
  93: - user-driven commits and pushes
  94: - reading MemoryTree outputs after they are copied back from the memory worktree
  95: 
  96: It must not run automatic heartbeat commits.
  97: 
  98: ### Memory Worktree
  99: 
 100: The memory worktree is a separate local folder created with `git worktree`.
 101: 
 102: Recommended characteristics:
 103: 
 104: - checked out on a dedicated branch, defaulting to `memorytree`
 105: - optionally allowed to use a custom branch name in detailed settings
 106: - registered as the project path used by heartbeat
 107: - allowed to run automatic MemoryTree writes, transcript imports, and report builds
 108: 
 109: Recommended path pattern:
 110: 
 111: - `~/.memorytree/worktrees/<repo-name>`
 112: 
 113: ## Why Worktree Instead Of Branch Merge
 114: 
 115: The intended model is not:
 116: 
 117: - run heartbeat on a side branch
 118: - merge that branch into the user's current development branch every 30 minutes
 119: 
 120: That approach would pollute branch history, create unnecessary merge commits, and mix MemoryTree automation with product-development Git flow.
 121: 
 122: The intended model is:
 123: 
 124: - keep heartbeat isolated in the memory worktree
 125: - copy selected MemoryTree files into the development directory
 126: - let the user decide how and when those files are committed on their active branch
 127: 
 128: This is directory-level synchronization, not branch-level merge automation.
 129: 
 130: ## Sync Model
 131: 
 132: ### Source Of Truth Rules
 133: 
 134: To avoid conflicts, each file group has only one source of truth.
 135: 
 136: #### Development Directory Is Source Of Truth
 137: 
 138: These files should be pushed from the development directory into the memory worktree before heartbeat runs:
 139: 
 140: - `AGENTS.md`
 141: - `Memory/01_goals/**`
 142: - `Memory/02_todos/**`
 143: - `Memory/03_chat_logs/**`
 144: - `Memory/04_knowledge/**`
 145: - `Memory/05_archive/**`
 146: 
 147: Reason:
 148: 
 149: These files reflect user-confirmed project context and active session state.
 150: 
 151: #### Memory Worktree Is Source Of Truth
 152: 
 153: These files should be copied from the memory worktree back into the development directory after heartbeat runs:
 154: 
 155: - `Memory/06_transcripts/**`
 156: - `Memory/07_reports/**`
 157: 
 158: Reason:
 159: 
 160: These files are the direct products of heartbeat-driven transcript import and report generation.
 161: 
 162: ### Visibility And Commit Semantics
 163: 
 164: When `Memory/06_transcripts/**` and `Memory/07_reports/**` are copied back into the development directory, they are expected to be visible to the user there.
 165: 
 166: `Memory/06_transcripts/**` is also expected to be committable from the development directory when the user decides that those files belong in the current branch.
 167: 
 168: This is an intentional product behavior, not a synchronization accident.
 169: 
 170: The only explicit exception remains any raw-transcript path that the repository policy keeps out of automatic staging until the user approves uploads.
 171: 
 172: ### Explicit Non-Goal
 173: 
 174: The same path must not be edited independently in both directories and then merged heuristically.
 175: 
 176: This upgrade should use directional copy rules, not two-way merge logic.
 177: 
 178: ## Sync Triggers
 179: 
 180: ### Background Sync
 181: 
 182: Recommended default behavior:
 183: 
 184: 1. One global heartbeat task runs on a short fixed interval.
 185: 2. Each project decides independently whether its own heartbeat interval has elapsed, for example `5m`.
 186: 3. A development-directory refresh runs on a slower per-project interval, for example `30m`.
 187: 4. That refresh copies the latest `Memory/06_transcripts/**` and `Memory/07_reports/**` from the memory worktree into the development directory.
 188: 
 189: This allows the user to see current MemoryTree data locally without running heartbeat in the development directory.
 190: 
 191: ### Manual Trigger
 192: 
 193: When the user manually triggers heartbeat from the directory they are currently editing, the system should perform the following sequence:
 194: 
 195: 1. Detect the current development directory.
 196: 2. Copy `AGENTS.md` and `Memory/01-05` from the development directory to the memory worktree.
 197: 3. Run heartbeat in the memory worktree.
 198: 4. Copy `Memory/06-07` from the memory worktree back to the current development directory.
 199: 
 200: This keeps the memory worktree aligned with the user's latest active context before running import and build operations.
 201: 
 202: ## Git Rules
 203: 
 204: ### Memory Worktree
 205: 
 206: The memory worktree may:
 207: 
 208: - run heartbeat
 209: - auto-import transcripts
 210: - auto-build reports
 211: - auto-commit MemoryTree-owned changes
 212: - auto-push its dedicated MemoryTree branch if enabled
 213: 
 214: ### Development Directory
 215: 
 216: The development directory must not:
 217: 
 218: - run automatic heartbeat commits
 219: - receive automatic branch merges from the memory worktree
 220: - have its normal branch protection or review rules bypassed
 221: 
 222: All GitHub upload behavior for the development directory remains under the user's existing branch logic.
 223: 
 224: Files copied back into the development directory may appear in `git status`, and that is expected.
 225: 
 226: ## Onboarding Flow
 227: 
 228: The intended activation flow should be:
 229: 
 230: 1. User enables MemoryTree for a repository.
 231: 2. System detects that the repository is not yet backed by a dedicated memory worktree.
 232: 3. System offers:
 233:    - `Quick Start`
 234:    - `Detailed Settings`
 235: 4. On confirmation, the system:
 236:    - creates or reuses a dedicated Git worktree
 237:    - checks out a dedicated MemoryTree branch inside that worktree
 238:    - registers the worktree path in `~/.memorytree/config.toml`
 239:    - preserves the user's development directory as the non-heartbeat working copy
 240: 
 241: ### Quick Start
 242: 
 243: Recommended defaults:
 244: 
 245: - global scheduler already installed or installed once during activation
 246: - project heartbeat interval: `5m`
 247: - project development-directory refresh interval: `30m`
 248: - project `auto_push = true`
 249: - project `generate_report = true`
 250: - project memory branch: `memorytree`
 251: - worktree-backed heartbeat enabled
 252: 
 253: ### Detailed Settings
 254: 
 255: The user may customize:
 256: 
 257: - project heartbeat interval
 258: - project development-directory refresh interval
 259: - project auto-push behavior
 260: - project report generation
 261: - project report publishing settings
 262: - dedicated memory branch name
 263: - raw transcript upload permission
 264: 
 265: ## Implementation Phases
 266: 
 267: ### Phase 1: Config Model
 268: 
 269: Add explicit configuration for:
 270: 
 271: - stable project identifier
 272: - development directory path
 273: - memory worktree path
 274: - project heartbeat interval
 275: - project development-directory refresh interval
 276: - project sync mode flags
 277: - project auto-push and report settings
 278: - per-project report publishing settings
 279: - last-run timestamps needed for due-check evaluation under one global scheduler
 280: 
 281: Example shape:
 282: 
 283: ```toml
 284: heartbeat_tick = "1m"
 285: 
 286: [[projects]]
 287: id = "memorytree-workflow"
 288: development_path = "D:/demo1/memorytree-workflow"
 289: memory_path = "C:/Users/ai/.memorytree/worktrees/memorytree-workflow"
 290: memory_branch = "memorytree"
 291: heartbeat_interval = "5m"
 292: refresh_interval = "30m"
 293: auto_push = true
 294: generate_report = true
 295: report_port = 10010
 296: gh_pages_branch = "gh-pages"
 297: report_base_url = "https://example.github.io/memorytree-workflow"
 298: ```
 299: 
 300: ### Phase 2: Worktree Bootstrap
 301: 
 302: Add a bootstrap step that:
 303: 
 304: - verifies the repository is Git-backed
 305: - creates or reuses a dedicated worktree
 306: - ensures the worktree is on the project's configured MemoryTree branch, defaulting to `memorytree`
 307: 
 308: ### Phase 3: Directional Sync Engine
 309: 
 310: Add a file sync mechanism with whitelist-based copy rules:
 311: 
 312: - development directory to worktree for `AGENTS.md` and `Memory/01-05`
 313: - worktree to development directory for `Memory/06-07`
 314: 
 315: ### Phase 4: Manual Trigger Integration
 316: 
 317: Wire manual heartbeat execution so it always:
 318: 
 319: 1. syncs latest context into the worktree
 320: 2. runs heartbeat there
 321: 3. syncs outputs back to the invoking development directory
 322: 
 323: ### Phase 5: Background Refresh
 324: 
 325: Add a scheduled refresh path that copies worktree outputs back into the development directory on a slower cadence than heartbeat.
 326: 
 327: ## Current CLI Surface
 328: 
 329: The current implementation now exposes two concrete entry points for this design:
 330: 
 331: 1. `memorytree daemon register --root <repo> --quick-start`
 332: 2. `memorytree daemon register --root <repo> --heartbeat-interval <x> --refresh-interval <y> --auto-push <true|false> --generate-report <true|false> --branch <name>`
 333: 
 334: What `daemon register` now does:
 335: 
 336: - registers or updates the project in `~/.memorytree/config.toml`
 337: - writes both `development_path` and `memory_path`
 338: - creates or reuses the dedicated Git worktree
 339: - stores `memory_branch` per project
 340: - ensures the worktree is on the configured branch
 341: - uses `memorytree` for Quick Start by default, while detailed settings may override the branch name
 342: - auto-configures the first upstream binding when `auto_push` is enabled and a remote exists
 343: 
 344: The current implementation also extends manual execution:
 345: 
 346: - `memorytree daemon run-once --root <repo> --force`
 347: 
 348: That command targets the matching project directly, bypasses due checks, and runs the worktree-backed sync-and-heartbeat flow immediately.
 349: 
 350: ## Risks And Guardrails
 351: 
 352: ### Risk: Conflicting Edits
 353: 
 354: If both directories edit the same file family, the system can overwrite newer local content.
 355: 
 356: Guardrail:
 357: 
 358: - keep strict source-of-truth ownership by directory
 359: 
 360: ### Risk: Hidden Sync Failures
 361: 
 362: If copy steps fail silently, the user may believe heartbeat is working while the development directory stays stale.
 363: 
 364: Guardrail:
 365: 
 366: - surface sync status in logs and alerts
 367: 
 368: ### Risk: Wrong Registered Path
 369: 
 370: If heartbeat remains registered against the main development directory instead of the worktree, the isolation model breaks.
 371: 
 372: Guardrail:
 373: 
 374: - store and display both paths explicitly during activation and status checks
 375: 
 376: ### Risk: Cross-Project Identity Collisions
 377: 
 378: If project identity depends only on directory basename or slug matching, similarly named repositories can interfere with routing or transcript matching.
 379: 
 380: Guardrail:
 381: 
 382: - add a stable project identifier in project configuration
 383: - prefer exact configured paths over inferred names whenever possible
 384: 
 385: ## Current Recommendation
 386: 
 387: Use a dedicated memory worktree as the only heartbeat execution target.
 388: 
 389: Do not merge the MemoryTree branch into the user's active development branch on a timer.
 390: 
 391: Instead, copy MemoryTree output files from the worktree back into the development directory so the user can read them and decide how they participate in the normal Git workflow.
 392: 
 393: Keep the scheduler global, but make runtime behavior project-specific.

 123: 
 124: - keep heartbeat isolated in the memory worktree
 125: - copy selected MemoryTree files into the development directory
 126: - let the user decide how and when those files are committed on their active branch
 127: 
 128: This is directory-level synchronization, not branch-level merge automation.
 129: 
 130: ## Sync Model
 131: 
 132: ### Source Of Truth Rules
 133: 
 134: To avoid conflicts, each file group has only one source of truth.
 135: 
 136: #### Development Directory Is Source Of Truth
 137: 
 138: These files should be pushed from the development directory into the memory worktree before heartbeat runs:
 139: 
 140: - `AGENTS.md`
 141: - `Memory/01_goals/**`
 142: - `Memory/02_todos/**`
 143: - `Memory/03_chat_logs/**`
 144: - `Memory/04_knowledge/**`
 145: - `Memory/05_archive/**`
 146: 
 147: Reason:
 148: 
 149: These files reflect user-confirmed project context and active session state.
 150: 
 151: #### Memory Worktree Is Source Of Truth
 152: 
 153: These files should be copied from the memory worktree back into the development directory after heartbeat runs:
 154: 
 155: - `Memory/06_transcripts/**`
 156: - `Memory/07_reports/**`
 157: 
 158: Reason:
 159: 
 160: These files are the direct products of heartbeat-driven transcript import and report generation.
 161: 
 162: ### Visibility And Commit Semantics
 163: 
 164: When `Memory/06_transcripts/**` and `Memory/07_reports/**` are copied back into the development directory, they are expected to be visible to the user there.
 165: 
 166: `Memory/06_transcripts/**` is also expected to be committable from the development directory when the user decides that those files belong in the current branch.
 167: 
 168: This is an intentional product behavior, not a synchronization accident.
 169: 
 170: The only explicit exception remains any raw-transcript path that the repository policy keeps out of automatic staging until the user approves uploads.
 171: 
 172: ### Explicit Non-Goal
 173: 
 174: The same path must not be edited independently in both directories and then merged heuristically.
 175: 
 176: This upgrade should use directional copy rules, not two-way merge logic.
 177: 
 178: ## Sync Triggers
 179: 
 180: ### Background Sync
 181: 
 182: Recommended default behavior:
 183: 
 184: 1. One global heartbeat task runs on a short fixed interval.
 185: 2. Each project decides independently whether its own heartbeat interval has elapsed, for example `5m`.
 186: 3. A development-directory refresh runs on a slower per-project interval, for example `30m`.
 187: 4. That refresh copies the latest `Memory/06_transcripts/**` and `Memory/07_reports/**` from the memory worktree into the development directory.
 188: 
 189: This allows the user to see current MemoryTree data locally without running heartbeat in the development directory.
 190: 
 191: ### Manual Trigger
 192: 
 193: When the user manually triggers heartbeat from the directory they are currently editing, the system should perform the following sequence:
 194: 
 195: 1. Detect the current development directory.
 196: 2. Copy `AGENTS.md` and `Memory/01-05` from the development directory to the memory worktree.
 197: 3. Run heartbeat in the memory worktree.
 198: 4. Copy `Memory/06-07` from the memory worktree back to the current development directory.
 199: 
 200: This keeps the memory worktree aligned with the user's latest active context before running import and build operations.
 201: 
 202: ## Git Rules
 203: 
 204: ### Memory Worktree
 205: 
 206: The memory worktree may:
 207: 
 208: - run heartbeat
 209: - auto-import transcripts
 210: - auto-build reports
 211: - auto-commit MemoryTree-owned changes
 212: - auto-push its dedicated MemoryTree branch if enabled
 213: 
 214: ### Development Directory
 215: 
 216: The development directory must not:
 217: 
 218: - run automatic heartbeat commits
 219: - receive automatic branch merges from the memory worktree
 220: - have its normal branch protection or review rules bypassed
 221: 
 222: All GitHub upload behavior for the development directory remains under the user's existing branch logic.
 223: 
 224: Files copied back into the development directory may appear in `git status`, and that is expected.
 225: 
 226: ## Onboarding Flow
 227: 
 228: The intended activation flow should be:
 229: 
 230: 1. User enables MemoryTree for a repository.
 231: 2. System detects that the repository is not yet backed by a dedicated memory worktree.
 232: 3. System offers:
 233:    - `Quick Start`
 234:    - `Detailed Settings`
 235: 4. On confirmation, the system:
 236:    - creates or reuses a dedicated Git worktree
 237:    - checks out a dedicated MemoryTree branch inside that worktree
 238:    - registers the worktree path in `~/.memorytree/config.toml`
 239:    - preserves the user's development directory as the non-heartbeat working copy
 240: 
 241: ### Quick Start
 242: 
 243: Recommended defaults:
 244: 
 245: - global scheduler already installed or installed once during activation
 246: - project heartbeat interval: `5m`
 247: - project development-directory refresh interval: `30m`
 248: - project `auto_push = true`
 249: - project `generate_report = true`
 250: - project memory branch: `memorytree`
 251: - worktree-backed heartbeat enabled
 252: 
 253: ### Detailed Settings
 254: 
 255: The user may customize:
 256: 
 257: - project heartbeat interval
 258: - project development-directory refresh interval
 259: - project auto-push behavior
 260: - project report generation
 261: - project report publishing settings
 262: - dedicated memory branch name
 263: - raw transcript upload permission
 264: 
 265: ## Implementation Phases
 266: 
 267: ### Phase 1: Config Model
 268: 
 269: Add explicit configuration for:
 270: 
 271: - stable project identifier
 272: - development directory path
 273: - memory worktree path
 274: - project heartbeat interval
 275: - project development-directory refresh interval
 276: - project sync mode flags
 277: - project auto-push and report settings
 278: - per-project report publishing settings
 279: - last-run timestamps needed for due-check evaluation under one global scheduler
 280: 
 281: Example shape:
 282: 
 283: ```toml
 284: heartbeat_tick = "1m"
 285: 
 286: [[projects]]
 287: id = "memorytree-workflow"
 288: development_path = "D:/demo1/memorytree-workflow"
 289: memory_path = "C:/Users/ai/.memorytree/worktrees/memorytree-workflow"
 290: memory_branch = "memorytree"
 291: heartbeat_interval = "5m"
 292: refresh_interval = "30m"
 293: auto_push = true
 294: generate_report = true
 295: report_port = 10010
 296: gh_pages_branch = "gh-pages"
 297: report_base_url = "https://example.github.io/memorytree-workflow"
 298: ```
 299: 
 300: ### Phase 2: Worktree Bootstrap
 301: 
 302: Add a bootstrap step that:
 303: 
 304: - verifies the repository is Git-backed
 305: - creates or reuses a dedicated worktree
 306: - ensures the worktree is on the project's configured MemoryTree branch, defaulting to `memorytree`
 307: 
 308: ### Phase 3: Directional Sync Engine
 309: 
 310: Add a file sync mechanism with whitelist-based copy rules:
 311: 
 312: - development directory to worktree for `AGENTS.md` and `Memory/01-05`
 313: - worktree to development directory for `Memory/06-07`
 314: 
 315: ### Phase 4: Manual Trigger Integration
 316: 
 317: Wire manual heartbeat execution so it always:
 318: 
 319: 1. syncs latest context into the worktree
 320: 2. runs heartbeat there
 321: 3. syncs outputs back to the invoking development directory
 322: 
 323: ### Phase 5: Background Refresh
 324: 
 325: Add a scheduled refresh path that copies worktree outputs back into the development directory on a slower cadence than heartbeat.
 326: 
 327: ## Current CLI Surface
 328: 
 329: The current implementation now exposes two concrete entry points for this design:
 330: 
 331: 1. `memorytree daemon register --root <repo> --quick-start`
 332: 2. `memorytree daemon register --root <repo> --heartbeat-interval <x> --refresh-interval <y> --auto-push <true|false> --generate-report <true|false> --branch <name>`
 333: 
 334: What `daemon register` now does:
 335: 
 336: - registers or updates the project in `~/.memorytree/config.toml`
 337: - writes both `development_path` and `memory_path`
 338: - creates or reuses the dedicated Git worktree
 339: - stores `memory_branch` per project
 340: - ensures the worktree is on the configured branch
 341: - uses `memorytree` for Quick Start by default, while detailed settings may override the branch name
 342: - auto-configures the first upstream binding when `auto_push` is enabled and a remote exists
 343: 
 344: The current implementation also extends manual execution:
 345: 
 346: - `memorytree daemon run-once --root <repo> --force`
 347: 
 348: That command targets the matching project directly, bypasses due checks, and runs the worktree-backed sync-and-heartbeat flow immediately.
 349: 
 350: ## Risks And Guardrails
 351: 
 352: ### Risk: Conflicting Edits
 353: 
 354: If both directories edit the same file family, the system can overwrite newer local content.
 355: 
 356: Guardrail:
 357: 
 358: - keep strict source-of-truth ownership by directory
 359: 
 360: ### Risk: Hidden Sync Failures
 361: 
 362: If copy steps fail silently, the user may believe heartbeat is working while the development directory stays stale.
 363: 
 364: Guardrail:
 365: 
 366: - surface sync status in logs and alerts
 367: 
 368: ### Risk: Wrong Registered Path
 369: 
 370: If heartbeat remains registered against the main development directory instead of the worktree, the isolation model breaks.
 371: 
 372: Guardrail:
 373: 
 374: - store and display both paths explicitly during activation and status checks
 375: 
 376: ### Risk: Cross-Project Identity Collisions
 377: 
 378: If project identity depends only on directory basename or slug matching, similarly named repositories can interfere with routing or transcript matching.
 379: 
 380: Guardrail:
 381: 
 382: - add a stable project identifier in project configuration
 383: - prefer exact configured paths over inferred names whenever possible
 384: 
 385: ## Current Recommendation
 386: 
 387: Use a dedicated memory worktree as the only heartbeat execution target.
 388: 
 389: Do not merge the MemoryTree branch into the user's active development branch on a timer.
 390: 
 391: Instead, copy MemoryTree output files from the worktree back into the development directory so the user can read them and decide how they participate in the normal Git workflow.
 392: 
 393: Keep the scheduler global, but make runtime behavior project-specific.

events.text:
按你刚刚定下来的方向，我先把新模型冻结成一句话：

`memorytree` 分支和它对应的 worktree 是唯一的“可共享、可传播、可持久保存”的记忆源；开发目录里的 `AGENTS.md` 和 `Memory/**` 都只是本地缓存镜像，不承担正式 Git 共享职责。我先按“没有仅本地保留、但不进入 `memorytree` 分支的特殊内容”来理解你第 2 点。

**需要一起改口的地方**
我查了一遍，当前最明显与新模型冲突的文件有这些：

- [README.md](D:/demo1/memorytree-workflow/README.md:7)  
  现在还把产品定义成 `Git-tracked project memory`，并且在 [README.md](D:/demo1/memorytree-workflow/README.md:293)、[README.md](D:/demo1/memorytree-workflow/README.md:372)、[README.md](D:/demo1/memorytree-workflow/README.md:390) 明确保留了 `refresh_interval = 30m` 的语义。
- [docs/worktree-heartbeat-upgrade-plan.md](D:/demo1/memorytree-workflow/docs/worktree-heartbeat-upgrade-plan.md:164)  
  这里明确写了开发目录里的 `Memory/06_transcripts/**` “可见且可提交”，并在 [docs/worktree-heartbeat-upgrade-plan.md](D:/demo1/memorytree-workflow/docs/worktree-heartbeat-upgrade-plan.md:186) 继续保留了 `30m refresh` 的产品设计，这和你现在的新模型正面冲突。
- [references/git-policy.md](D:/demo1/memorytree-workflow/references/git-policy.md:27)  
  这里仍把 `Memory/**` 和部分 `AGENTS.md` 视为 repo PR 的默认白名单，等于默认它们应该属于正常仓库提交流程。
- [references/update-rules.md](D:/demo1/memorytree-workflow/references/update-rules.md:48) 和 [references/transcript-archive.md](D:/demo1/memorytree-workflow/references/transcript-archive.md:159)  
  这两份文档还在说 repo mirror 要保留、raw 是否能 push 取决于审批，语义还是“主仓库 mirror 可进 Git”，不是“主目录只是缓存层”。
- [src/heartbeat/sync.ts](D:/demo1/memorytree-workflow/src/heartbeat/sync.ts:6)  
  当前同步模型仍是“开发目录为 `AGENTS.md` + `Memory/01-05` 源头，worktree 为 `Memory/06-07` 源头”，不是“开发目录完整缓存 mirror”。
- [src/heartbeat/heartbeat.ts](D:/demo1/memorytree-workflow/src/heartbeat/heartbeat.ts:301)  
  自动提交只扫 `Memory/`，并不包含 `AGENTS.md`，所以现在还没做到“全部记忆都进入 `memorytree` 分支”。
- [src/cli/cmd-daemon.ts](D:/demo1/memorytree-workflow/src/cli/cmd-daemon.ts:38) 和 [src/heartbeat/config.ts](D:/demo1/memorytree-workflow/src/heartbeat/config.ts:19)  
  代码和配置模型仍把 `refresh_interval = 30m` 当正式能力。

**如果写进项目 todo，我建议按从易到难排成这样**
1. 明确产品定义  
   把“`memorytree` 分支是唯一共享记忆源，开发目录是缓存镜像层”写成正式产品规则。
2. 文档统一改口  
   先改 [README.md](D:/demo1/memorytree-workflow/README.md)、[docs/worktree-heartbeat-upgrade-plan.md](D:/demo1/memorytree-workflow/docs/worktree-heartbeat-upgrade-plan.md)、[references/git-policy.md](D:/demo1/memorytree-workflow/references/git-policy.md)、[references/update-rules.md](D:/demo1/memorytree-workflow/references/update-rules.md)、[references/transcript-archive.md](D:/demo1/memorytree-workflow/references/transcript-archive.md)。
3. 去掉 `30m refresh` 这个产品概念  
   从 README、CLI help、配置说明、设计文档里移除 `refresh_interval` 的对外语义。
4. 明确主目录 Git 策略  
   决定开发目录里的 `AGENTS.md` 和 `Memory/**` 是否全部 `.gitignore`，以及已被跟踪文件如何安全退跟踪。
5. 补齐 `memorytree` 分支的提交闭环  
   让 heartbeat 自动提交范围从“仅 `Memory/`”扩展到“`AGENTS.md` + 全部 `Memory/**`”。
6. 补齐 raw transcript 策略  
   既然你现在倾向“所有的都进入”，那就要决定 raw 也是否跟着自动提交到 `memorytree` 分支，并同步调整权限逻辑。
7. 调整 heartbeat 执行顺序  
   现在是先 commit/push，再 build report；以后要改成“生成完整状态后一次性提交”，否则 `memorytree` 分支仍然不是完整快照。
8. 重写同步模型  
   从“01-05/06-07 分治”改成“开发目录是完整缓存镜像”，这会动到 [sync.ts](D:/demo1/memorytree-workflow/src/heartbeat/sync.ts) 的源头规则。
9. 设计退跟踪迁移方案  
   这不是简单加 ignore，要考虑已跟踪文件、用户本地文件保留、历史兼容、升级路径。
10. 全量测试与迁移验证  
   CLI、heartbeat、upgrade、report、e2e、跨平台行为都要跟着重测。

按公允判断，你这个新方向是成立的，而且逻辑比现在更统一。真正难的不是“该不该这么做”，而是第 7 到第 10 项：一旦正式落地，它会从“worktree 隔离 + 部分镜像”升级成“memorytree 分支单一真源 + 开发目录纯缓存”的完整模型切换。

如果你愿意，我下一步继续只讨论不改代码，直接把这 10 条再压成“第一阶段必须做 / 第二阶段再做”的实施顺序。

`memorytree` 分支和它对应的 worktree 是唯一的“可共享、可传播、可持久保存”的记忆源；开发目录里的 `AGENTS.md` 和 `Memory/**` 都只是本地缓存镜像，不承担正式 Git 共享职责。我先按“没有仅本地保留、但不进入 `memorytree` 分支的特殊内容”来理解你第 2 点。

**需要一起改口的地方**
我查了一遍，当前最明显与新模型冲突的文件有这些：

- [README.md](D:/demo1/memorytree-workflow/README.md:7)  
  现在还把产品定义成 `Git-tracked project memory`，并且在 [README.md](D:/demo1/memorytree-workflow/README.md:293)、[README.md](D:/demo1/memorytree-workflow/README.md:372)、[README.md](D:/demo1/memorytree-workflow/README.md:390) 明确保留了 `refresh_interval = 30m` 的语义。
- [docs/worktree-heartbeat-upgrade-plan.md](D:/demo1/memorytree-workflow/docs/worktree-heartbeat-upgrade-plan.md:164)  
  这里明确写了开发目录里的 `Memory/06_transcripts/**` “可见且可提交”，并在 [docs/worktree-heartbeat-upgrade-plan.md](D:/demo1/memorytree-workflow/docs/worktree-heartbeat-upgrade-plan.md:186) 继续保留了 `30m refresh` 的产品设计，这和你现在的新模型正面冲突。
- [references/git-policy.md](D:/demo1/memorytree-workflow/references/git-policy.md:27)  
  这里仍把 `Memory/**` 和部分 `AGENTS.md` 视为 repo PR 的默认白名单，等于默认它们应该属于正常仓库提交流程。
- [references/update-rules.md](D:/demo1/memorytree-workflow/references/update-rules.md:48) 和 [references/transcript-archive.md](D:/demo1/memorytree-workflow/references/transcript-archive.md:159)  
  这两份文档还在说 repo mirror 要保留、raw 是否能 push 取决于审批，语义还是“主仓库 mirror 可进 Git”，不是“主目录只是缓存层”。
- [src/heartbeat/sync.ts](D:/demo1/memorytree-workflow/src/heartbeat/sync.ts:6)  
  当前同步模型仍是“开发目录为 `AGENTS.md` + `Memory/01-05` 源头，worktree 为 `Memory/06-07` 源头”，不是“开发目录完整缓存 mirror”。
- [src/heartbeat/heartbeat.ts](D:/demo1/memorytree-workflow/src/heartbeat/heartbeat.ts:301)  
  自动提交只扫 `Memory/`，并不包含 `AGENTS.md`，所以现在还没做到“全部记忆都进入 `memorytree` 分支”。
- [src/cli/cmd-daemon.ts](D:/demo1/memorytree-workflow/src/cli/cmd-daemon.ts:38) 和 [src/heartbeat/config.ts](D:/demo1/memorytree-workflow/src/heartbeat/config.ts:19)  
  代码和配置模型仍把 `refresh_interval = 30m` 当正式能力。

**如果写进项目 todo，我建议按从易到难排成这样**
1. 明确产品定义  
   把“`memorytree` 分支是唯一共享记忆源，开发目录是缓存镜像层”写成正式产品规则。
2. 文档统一改口  
   先改 [README.md](D:/demo1/memorytree-workflow/README.md)、[docs/worktree-heartbeat-upgrade-plan.md](D:/demo1/memorytree-workflow/docs/worktree-heartbeat-upgrade-plan.md)、[references/git-policy.md](D:/demo1/memorytree-workflow/references/git-policy.md)、[references/update-rules.md](D:/demo1/memorytree-workflow/references/update-rules.md)、[references/transcript-archive.md](D:/demo1/memorytree-workflow/references/transcript-archive.md)。
3. 去掉 `30m refresh` 这个产品概念  
   从 README、CLI help、配置说明、设计文档里移除 `refresh_interval` 的对外语义。
4. 明确主目录 Git 策略  
   决定开发目录里的 `AGENTS.md` 和 `Memory/**` 是否全部 `.gitignore`，以及已被跟踪文件如何安全退跟踪。
5. 补齐 `memorytree` 分支的提交闭环  
   让 heartbeat 自动提交范围从“仅 `Memory/`”扩展到“`AGENTS.md` + 全部 `Memory/**`”。
6. 补齐 raw transcript 策略  
   既然你现在倾向“所有的都进入”，那就要决定 raw 也是否跟着自动提交到 `memorytree` 分支，并同步调整权限逻辑。
7. 调整 heartbeat 执行顺序  
   现在是先 commit/push，再 build report；以后要改成“生成完整状态后一次性提交”，否则 `memorytree` 分支仍然不是完整快照。
8. 重写同步模型  
   从“01-05/06-07 分治”改成“开发目录是完整缓存镜像”，这会动到 [sync.ts](D:/demo1/memorytree-workflow/src/heartbeat/sync.ts) 的源头规则。
9. 设计退跟踪迁移方案  
   这不是简单加 ignore，要考虑已跟踪文件、用户本地文件保留、历史兼容、升级路径。
10. 全量测试与迁移验证  
   CLI、heartbeat、upgrade、report、e2e、跨平台行为都要跟着重测。

按公允判断，你这个新方向是成立的，而且逻辑比现在更统一。真正难的不是“该不该这么做”，而是第 7 到第 10 项：一旦正式落地，它会从“worktree 隔离 + 部分镜像”升级成“memorytree 分支单一真源 + 开发目录纯缓存”的完整模型切换。

如果你愿意，我下一步继续只讨论不改代码，直接把这 10 条再压成“第一阶段必须做 / 第二阶段再做”的实施顺序。

settings.developer_instructions:
# Collaboration Mode: Default

You are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.

Your active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.

## request_user_input availability

The `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.

In Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.

metadata.user_instructions:
# AGENTS.md

## Read Order
- Read this file first.
- Read the latest file in `Memory/01_goals/`.
- Read the latest todo bound to that goal in `Memory/02_todos/`.
- Read `Memory/04_knowledge/` only when needed.
- Read `Memory/06_transcripts/clean/` and transcript manifests only when needed.
- Read `Memory/03_chat_logs/` and `Memory/05_archive/` only when needed.

## Memory Rules
- Change the active goal only after explicit user confirmation of a requirement or scope change.
- Keep the active todo synchronized with the active goal.
- Append chat logs only. Never rewrite or delete prior entries.
- Keep active files concise and move stale context to knowledge or archive files.
- Treat cleaned transcript indexes as search aids. Confirm exact wording against the raw transcript archive when precision matters.

## Transcript Rules
- If transcript archival is enabled, archive transcripts by client, project, and session.
- Keep raw transcripts in both this repository's `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/` mirror and the user-level MemoryTree archive.
- Keep this repository mirror limited to transcripts that belong to the current project. Archive unrelated projects in the global MemoryTree archive only.
- Generate cleaned transcript indexes with deterministic code, not model-generated rewriting.
- Raw transcript upload permission for this repository: approved on 2026-03-21.
- If raw transcript uploads are not approved, keep `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` unstaged and commit only cleaned transcript indexes or manifests for this repository.

## Git Rules
- Obey this repository's branch, PR, CI, review, and release rules.
- Auto-commit and push only MemoryTree-owned changes (`Memory/**` and this file when it is managed by MemoryTree), excluding `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` until raw transcript uploads are approved for this repository.
- Use a MemoryTree-scoped commit title. Prefer `memorytree(<scope>): <subject>`, or the repository-required equivalent such as `docs(memorytree): <subject>`.
- Use a dedicated branch and PR for MemoryTree-only changes. Enable auto-merge only when repository rules permit it.
- If a diff includes product code, shared policy files, cross-project transcript files, or unclear ownership, stop and ask the user before staging or pushing.
- When `auto_push` is enabled, the heartbeat process pushes automatically after committing. If no remote is configured or a push fails, an alert is written to `~/.memorytree/alerts.json`.

## Heartbeat Rules
- Transcript discovery, import, cleaning, and push are handled by the background heartbeat process. The model does not execute these operations.
- The model only writes chat log summaries and updates goals and todos during interactive sessions.
- If `~/.memorytree/alerts.json` contains pending notifications, display them at the start of the session.
- If `memorytree-daemon` is available but not registered on this machine, ask the user for their preferred settings and offer to run `memorytree-daemon install`.
- When the user asks to see their most recent conversation, trigger an on-demand sync for the current project, locate the latest session across all clients (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI), and generate a continuation summary.
- All interactive prompts must use plain-text questions. Do not rely on client-specific UI components.

events.text:
可以简化，但不能完全“不管”。

公允地说，你这个场景里：

- 还在开发阶段
- 只有你自己在用
- 还没形成稳定外部用户习惯

那第 9 项“设计复杂退跟踪迁移方案”确实可以降级，没必要做成很重的兼容工程。

但有一个底线不能跳过：

- 这些文件现在已经被 Git 跟踪
- 仅仅把它们写进 [`.gitignore`](D:/demo1/memorytree-workflow/.gitignore) 不会让 Git 自动停止跟踪
- 所以下一个版本如果要正式切换到“不跟踪”，仍然需要一次明确的“退跟踪提交”

也就是说，正确的公允判断是：

1. 可以不做复杂迁移方案。  
不需要考虑多用户兼容、老版本自动升级、平滑过渡脚本这些重东西。

2. 但不能完全不处理“已跟踪”这件事。  
至少要有一次明确版本切换，把这些路径从 Git index 里移除，并配合 `.gitignore` 固化新规则。

3. 如果你接受“历史里旧版本仍然看得到这些文件”，那就够了。  
因为你现在真正要的是“从下一个版本开始不再跟踪”，不是“清洗全部 Git 历史”。

所以第 9 项我建议改写成轻量版，不再叫“迁移方案设计”，而叫：

- 一次性退跟踪切换
- 验证下一个版本开始主目录不再跟踪 `AGENTS.md` 和 `Memory/**`
- 不处理旧历史重写

按这个新判断，我会把实施顺序改成两阶段：

**第一阶段必须做**
1. 定义新产品规则：`memorytree` 分支是唯一共享记忆源，开发目录是缓存镜像。
2. 去掉 `30m refresh` 的产品语义，统一成 `5m`。
3. 改 heartbeat 提交闭环，让 `AGENTS.md` + 全部 `Memory/**` 都能进入 `memorytree` 分支。
4. 调整执行顺序，确保 report 也在同轮进入 `memorytree` 分支。
5. 做主目录“一次性退跟踪切换”，并在 [`.gitignore`](D:/demo1/memorytree-workflow/.gitignore) 固化规则。
6. 更新 README、设计文档、Git policy、transcript 规则文档。

**第二阶段再做**
1. 清理或重构旧的 `refresh_interval` 配置字段和相关 CLI 帮助。
2. 重新整理 upgrade/init 文案，让新模型更清楚。
3. 做跨平台和 e2e 回归验证。
4. 视需要再决定是否处理旧历史中的敏感内容。

所以你的判断大体是对的：  
可以不做复杂迁移设计，但不能跳过那次“正式退跟踪”的版本切换。

如果你愿意，我下一步继续只讨论不修改，直接把“第一阶段必须做”的 6 条再细化成可执行 checklist。

- If raw transcript uploads are not approved, keep `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` unstaged and commit only cleaned transcript indexes or manifests for this repository.

## Git Rules
- Obey this repository's branch, PR, CI, review, and release rules.
- Auto-commit and push only MemoryTree-owned changes (`Memory/**` and this file when it is managed by MemoryTree), excluding `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` until raw transcript uploads are approved for this repository.
- Use a MemoryTree-scoped commit title. Prefer `memorytree(<scope>): <subject>`, or the repository-required equivalent such as `docs(memorytree): <subject>`.
- Use a dedicated branch and PR for MemoryTree-only changes. Enable auto-merge only when repository rules permit it.
- If a diff includes product code, shared policy files, cross-project transcript files, or unclear ownership, stop and ask the user before staging or pushing.
- When `auto_push` is enabled, the heartbeat process pushes automatically after committing. If no remote is configured or a push fails, an alert is written to `~/.memorytree/alerts.json`.

## Heartbeat Rules
- Transcript discovery, import, cleaning, and push are handled by the background heartbeat process. The model does not execute these operations.
- The model only writes chat log summaries and updates goals and todos during interactive sessions.
- If `~/.memorytree/alerts.json` contains pending notifications, display them at the start of the session.
- If `memorytree-daemon` is available but not registered on this machine, ask the user for their preferred settings and offer to run `memorytree-daemon install`.
- When the user asks to see their most recent conversation, trigger an on-demand sync for the current project, locate the latest session across all clients (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI), and generate a continuation summary.
- All interactive prompts must use plain-text questions. Do not rely on client-specific UI components.

可以，我们把第一阶段压成一份可执行 checklist，先只讨论，不改代码。

1. 锁定新产品规则  
   明确写死这三条：
   - `memorytree` 分支 + memory worktree 是唯一共享记忆源
   - 开发目录里的 `AGENTS.md` 和 `Memory/**` 只是本地缓存镜像
   - 主开发分支不再承担记忆的 Git 传播职责

2. 去掉 `30m refresh` 的产品语义  
   要处理的点：
   - 对外文档不再说 `refresh_interval = 30m`
   - Quick Start 不再宣传 `5m heartbeat + 30m refresh`
   - CLI help 不再把 `refresh` 当成推荐默认模型
   - 配置里即使暂时保留字段，也不能继续作为核心产品概念

3. 补齐 `memorytree` 分支的提交范围  
   当前还缺的闭环：
   - `AGENTS.md` 进入自动提交范围
   - `Memory/01-05` 确认是否在 heartbeat 中形成可提交更新
   - `Memory/06` 全量进入统一提交逻辑
   - raw transcript 若按你现在的决定“所有的都进入”，则要纳入 `memorytree` 分支策略

4. 调整 heartbeat 执行顺序  
   目标是让一轮 heartbeat 产出的完整状态一次性进入 `memorytree` 分支：
   - 先同步开发目录缓存到 worktree
   - 再导入 transcript
   - 再生成 report
   - 最后统一 commit/push  
   这样远程 `memorytree` 分支才是完整快照，而不是半成品。

5. 做主目录“一次性退跟踪切换”  
   目标不是重写历史，而是从下个版本开始改变规则：
   - 给 [`.gitignore`](D:/demo1/memorytree-workflow/.gitignore) 增加 `AGENTS.md` 和 `Memory/**` 规则
   - 把当前已跟踪的这些路径从 Git index 移除
   - 保留本地文件，不删除本地缓存
   - 验证之后 heartbeat 继续能写，但主目录默认不再参与正常 Git 提交

6. 统一文档与规则文件  
   至少要一起更新这些：
   - [README.md](D:/demo1/memorytree-workflow/README.md)
   - [docs/worktree-heartbeat-upgrade-plan.md](D:/demo1/memorytree-workflow/docs/worktree-heartbeat-upgrade-plan.md)
   - [references/git-policy.md](D:/demo1/memorytree-workflow/references/git-policy.md)
   - [references/update-rules.md](D:/demo1/memorytree-workflow/references/update-rules.md)
   - [references/transcript-archive.md](D:/demo1/memorytree-workflow/references/transcript-archive.md)  
   要统一改成：
   - 开发目录是缓存镜像
   - `memorytree` 分支是唯一共享源
   - 主目录默认不提交这些记忆文件
   - 不再主推 `30m refresh`

如果把这 6 条再按“最先做什么”排序，我建议是：

1. 先定规则  
2. 再改 heartbeat 提交闭环  
3. 再改执行顺序  
4. 再做退跟踪切换  
5. 最后统一文档

原因很简单：  
如果先退跟踪、后补 heartbeat 闭环，期间 `memorytree` 分支还没真正接住全部记忆，会出现短暂的不完整状态。

如果你愿意，我下一步还能继续只讨论不改代码，把第 3 和第 4 条再展开成“具体会改到哪些文件、哪些函数、哪些测试”。

settings.developer_instructions:
# Collaboration Mode: Default

You are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.

Your active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.

## request_user_input availability

The `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.

In Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.

metadata.user_instructions:
# AGENTS.md

## Read Order
- Read this file first.
- Read the latest file in `Memory/01_goals/`.
- Read the latest todo bound to that goal in `Memory/02_todos/`.
- Read `Memory/04_knowledge/` only when needed.
- Read `Memory/06_transcripts/clean/` and transcript manifests only when needed.
- Read `Memory/03_chat_logs/` and `Memory/05_archive/` only when needed.

## Memory Rules
- Change the active goal only after explicit user confirmation of a requirement or scope change.
- Keep the active todo synchronized with the active goal.
- Append chat logs only. Never rewrite or delete prior entries.
- Keep active files concise and move stale context to knowledge or archive files.
- Treat cleaned transcript indexes as search aids. Confirm exact wording against the raw transcript archive when precision matters.

## Transcript Rules
- If transcript archival is enabled, archive transcripts by client, project, and session.
- Keep raw transcripts in both this repository's `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/` mirror and the user-level MemoryTree archive.
- Keep this repository mirror limited to transcripts that belong to the current project. Archive unrelated projects in the global MemoryTree archive only.
- Generate cleaned transcript indexes with deterministic code, not model-generated rewriting.
- Raw transcript upload permission for this repository: approved on 2026-03-21.
- If raw transcript uploads are not approved, keep `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` unstaged and commit only cleaned transcript indexes or manifests for this repository.

## Git Rules
- Obey this repository's branch, PR, CI, review, and release rules.
- Auto-commit and push only MemoryTree-owned changes (`Memory/**` and this file when it is managed by MemoryTree), excluding `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` until raw transcript uploads are approved for this repository.
- Use a MemoryTree-scoped commit title. Prefer `memorytree(<scope>): <subject>`, or the repository-required equivalent such as `docs(memorytree): <subject>`.
- Use a dedicated branch and PR for MemoryTree-only changes. Enable auto-merge only when repository rules permit it.
- If a diff includes product code, shared policy files, cross-project transcript files, or unclear ownership, stop and ask the user before staging or pushing.
- When `auto_push` is enabled, the heartbeat process pushes automatically after committing. If no remote is configured or a push fails, an alert is written to `~/.memorytree/alerts.json`.

## Heartbeat Rules
- Transcript discovery, import, cleaning, and push are handled by the background heartbeat process. The model does not execute these operations.
- The model only writes chat log summaries and updates goals and todos during interactive sessions.
- If `~/.memorytree/alerts.json` contains pending notifications, display them at the start of the session.
- If `memorytree-daemon` is available but not registered on this machine, ask the user for their preferred settings and offer to run `memorytree-daemon install`.
- When the user asks to see their most recent conversation, trigger an on-demand sync for the current project, locate the latest session across all clients (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI), and generate a continuation summary.
- All interactive prompts must use plain-text questions. Do not rely on client-specific UI components.

Using Node v20.20.1
# AGENTS.md

## Read Order
- Read this file first.
- Read the latest file in `Memory/01_goals/`.
- Read the latest todo bound to that goal in `Memory/02_todos/`.
- Read `Memory/04_knowledge/` only when needed.
- Read `Memory/06_transcripts/clean/` and transcript manifests only when needed.
- Read `Memory/03_chat_logs/` and `Memory/05_archive/` only when needed.

## Memory Rules
- Change the active goal only after explicit user confirmation of a requirement or scope change.
- Keep the active todo synchronized with the active goal.
- Append chat logs only. Never rewrite or delete prior entries.
- Keep active files concise and move stale context to knowledge or archive files.
- Treat cleaned transcript indexes as search aids. Confirm exact wording against the raw transcript archive when precision matters.

## Transcript Rules
- If transcript archival is enabled, archive transcripts by client, project, and session.
- Keep raw transcripts in both this repository's `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/` mirror and the user-level MemoryTree archive.
- Keep this repository mirror limited to transcripts that belong to the current project. Archive unrelated projects in the global MemoryTree archive only.
- Generate cleaned transcript indexes with deterministic code, not model-generated rewriting.
- Raw transcript upload permission for this repository: approved on 2026-03-21.
- If raw transcript uploads are not approved, keep `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` unstaged and commit only cleaned transcript indexes or manifests for this repository.

## Git Rules
- Obey this repository's branch, PR, CI, review, and release rules.
- Auto-commit and push only MemoryTree-owned changes (`Memory/**` and this file when it is managed by MemoryTree), excluding `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` until raw transcript uploads are approved for this repository.
- Use a MemoryTree-scoped commit title. Prefer `memorytree(<scope>): <subject>`, or the repository-required equivalent such as `docs(memorytree): <subject>`.
- Use a dedicated branch and PR for MemoryTree-only changes. Enable auto-merge only when repository rules permit it.
- If a diff includes product code, shared policy files, cross-project transcript files, or unclear ownership, stop and ask the user before staging or pushing.
- When `auto_push` is enabled, the heartbeat process pushes automatically after committing. If no remote is configured or a push fails, an alert is written to `~/.memorytree/alerts.json`.

## Heartbeat Rules
- Transcript discovery, import, cleaning, and push are handled by the background heartbeat process. The model does not execute these operations.
- The model only writes chat log summaries and updates goals and todos during interactive sessions.
- If `~/.memorytree/alerts.json` contains pending notifications, display them at the start of the session.
- If `memorytree-daemon` is available but not registered on this machine, ask the user for their preferred settings and offer to run `memorytree-daemon install`.
- When the user asks to see their most recent conversation, trigger an on-demand sync for the current project, locate the latest session across all clients (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI), and generate a continuation summary.
- All interactive prompts must use plain-text questions. Do not rely on client-specific UI components.
