- Synced the local Codex `memorytree-workflow` skill wording to the current heartbeat model so it now treats `daemon quick-start` as the recommended current-repository setup path, keeps `daemon register` as the advanced customization path, and reserves `daemon install` for machine-level scheduler restore.
- Reinstalled the machine-level heartbeat scheduler on 2026-03-22 at a `5m` interval with `auto_push=true`, then verified the natural scheduled run at 17:07 completed successfully with `Last Result = 0` and a full `memorytree-workflow` import/push/report/sync cycle in the heartbeat log.
- User explicitly approved committing and pushing repo-local raw transcript mirrors for this repository on 2026-03-21, so `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` may now be included in dedicated MemoryTree-only Git branches and PRs.
- Fixed the remaining onboarding review gaps so `memorytree upgrade --format json` now prints the heartbeat quick-start hint to stderr without polluting JSON stdout, README now shows a truly custom advanced `daemon register` example instead of `--quick-start`, and the follow-up was re-verified with `npm test -- tests/cli/init-upgrade.test.ts`, `npm run typecheck`, `npm run build`, and `npm run test:e2e -- --run tests/e2e/cli-workflow.test.ts`.
- Clarified onboarding semantics so `init` and `upgrade` stay repository-only scaffold steps, `daemon quick-start` is the explicit quick-install path into heartbeat, and `daemon register` is framed as advanced setup; updated CLI help, next-step prompts, README wording, and tests to match.
- Merged the report hub so `Memory/07_reports/projects/index.html` now combines manifests from all registered projects (`memorytree-workflow`, `openmnemo`, and `papaweb`), and committed the change on `main` as `e062caa`.
- Verified local report access on `http://127.0.0.1:10010/`.
- Verified LAN access on `http://192.168.1.99:10010/`.
- Published the report to `gh-pages` and confirmed GitHub Pages at `https://beyondchenlin.github.io/memorytree-workflow/`.
- Fixed the scheduled publisher task and verified successful runs with `LastTaskResult = 0`.
- Drafted a worktree-based heartbeat upgrade document for isolating MemoryTree automation from the main development directory.
- Incorporated review decisions into the worktree upgrade document, including project-level config, a single global scheduler, and visible/committable transcript copies in the development directory.
- Implemented the project-level heartbeat config model with per-project heartbeat, refresh, report, and timestamp fields while keeping old TOML compatible.
- Implemented dedicated worktree bootstrap plus directional sync between development directories and MemoryTree worktrees.
- Added `memorytree daemon register` and targeted `memorytree daemon run-once --root <repo> --force` support for worktree-backed activation and manual execution.
- Verified the new worktree flow with unit tests, full unit suite, lint, typecheck, and CLI e2e coverage.
- Refined registration so Quick Start defaults the MemoryTree branch to `memorytree`, detailed settings can override it per project, and the first upstream binding is created automatically when `auto_push` is enabled.
- Added `memorytree daemon quick-start --root <repo>` as a one-command setup path that installs the scheduler when needed, registers the current repository with recommended defaults, and runs one immediate heartbeat sync.
- Added scenario-based `--help` guidance for `memorytree daemon`, `daemon install`, `daemon register`, `daemon run-once`, and `daemon quick-start`, then updated README and Chinese docs to recommend the new entry point.
- Verified the daemon simplification changes with `npm run typecheck`, `npm run lint`, `npm test`, and `npm run test:e2e`.
- Fixed two quick-start follow-up regressions: rerunning registration now preserves an existing repository's custom MemoryTree settings, and `daemon quick-start` now checks the installed scheduler cadence before recalibrating an already-installed but too-slow machine scheduler.
- Reframed local report access so Caddy is the primary long-running host, `report serve` is the temporary fallback path, and updated CLI help plus docs to match.
- Drafted a first-stage Caddy auto-management design document covering project-scoped Caddy config fragments, proposed `caddy enable|disable|status` commands, and conflict handling.
- Refined the Caddy auto-management design after review by locking it to a MemoryTree-owned main Caddyfile, adding a project-level report exposure rule with `local` as the safe default, and switching fragment naming to use stable `project.id` values.
- Implemented first-stage project-managed Caddy support with `memorytree caddy enable|disable|status`, a MemoryTree-owned `~/.memorytree/caddy/` config tree, project fragment filenames based on stable `project.id`, and local-only-by-default exposure via `report_exposure`.
- Added config parsing and roundtrip support for global and project-level `report_exposure`, plus unit coverage for config resolution, Caddy fragment rendering, command behavior, and CLI help output.
- Verified the Caddy phase-1 implementation with `npm run typecheck`, `npm run lint`, `npm test`, `npm run test:e2e -- --run tests/e2e/cli-workflow.test.ts`, and a real `caddy adapt` validation for both `local` and `lan` generated site blocks.
- Committed the Caddy phase-1 implementation as `f47643a` on `main` and pushed it to `origin/main`.
- Enabled the current repository in MemoryTree-managed Caddy and verified local HTTP access on `http://127.0.0.1:10010/` and `http://localhost:10010/`.
- Refreshed `README.md` after a full code walkthrough so it now reflects the current worktree-backed heartbeat architecture, managed Caddy flow, recommended `daemon quick-start` setup, configuration model, explicit `doubao` import path, and current validation coverage.
- Verified the current repository state after the README refresh with `npm run typecheck`, `npm run lint`, `npm run build`, `npm test`, and `npm run test:e2e`, then applied a small README header polish before preparing the docs update for GitHub.
- Committed the README refresh as `7269dc3` with title `docs(readme): refresh workflow overview` and pushed it to `origin/main` after the full validation stack passed.
- Expanded the GitHub Actions E2E workflow from Ubuntu-only to a three-platform matrix on Linux, macOS, and Windows, updated README validation wording to match, and re-verified the local Windows-side E2E suite with `npm run test:e2e`.
- Diagnosed the first PR failures as two separate cross-platform issues: a Windows-only absolute-path assertion in the Caddy manager test, and worktree rerun logic that mis-compared equivalent repository paths on macOS (`/var` vs `/private/var`) and Windows (short vs long path forms); fixed both and re-verified with `npm run typecheck`, `npm run lint`, `npm test`, and `npm run test:e2e`.
- Repaired the current repository's heartbeat branch conflict by renaming the stale `memorytree/memorytree-workflow` worktree branch to a dated backup branch, registering a fresh dedicated worktree on branch `memorytree` at `C:/Users/ai/.memorytree/worktrees/memorytree-workflow-active`, cleaning the duplicate Windows-case project entry in `~/.memorytree/config.toml`, and verifying `node dist/cli.js daemon run-once --root D:/demo1/memorytree-workflow --force` completes with transcript import, commit, push, report generation, and sync-back to the development directory.

## Pending
- No additional implementation tasks are pending for the approved first-stage Caddy auto-management scope.

events.input:
*** Begin Patch
*** Add File: d:\demo1\memorytree-workflow\docs\记忆分支单一真源两阶段改造方案.md
+# 记忆分支单一真源两阶段改造方案
+
+## 1. 背景
+
+当前 MemoryTree 的实现采用了「开发目录 + memory worktree + 全局归档」三层结构。
+
+它已经具备 worktree 隔离和 `memorytree` 分支自动提交能力，但产品语义仍然保留了两套承载方式：
+
+- `memorytree` 分支承担一部分共享记忆
+- 开发目录中的 `AGENTS.md` 和 `Memory/**` 仍被视为可见且可提交的仓库内容
+
+这会带来三个直接问题：
+
+- 共享记忆的唯一来源不清晰，容易形成双份承载
+- 开发目录容易出现敏感内容误提交
+- `5m heartbeat + 30m refresh` 的模型增加理解和维护成本
+
+## 2. 新目标
+
+本次改造要把产品模型统一为：
+
+- `memorytree` 分支和对应的 memory worktree 是唯一共享记忆源
+- 开发目录中的 `AGENTS.md` 和 `Memory/**` 全部都是本地缓存镜像
+- 开发目录缓存允许被 heartbeat 持续同步和覆盖，但默认不参与常规 Git 提交
+- heartbeat 的外部语义统一为固定短周期执行，不再对外强调单独的 `30m refresh` 模型
+- 所有 MemoryTree 内容最终都应完整进入 `memorytree` 分支，而不是只进入其中一部分
+
+## 3. 为什么要改
+
+### 3.1 降低误提交和敏感信息暴露风险
+
+当前主目录中的 `Memory/**` 很容易出现在 `git status` 中。只要用户习惯性提交，就可能把 transcript、报告和其他记忆内容一起推到正常开发分支。
+
+把开发目录改成缓存层之后，常规开发提交流程就不再默认携带这些内容。
+
+### 3.2 建立单一真源
+
+只要主目录和 `memorytree` 分支都在承担「正式记忆承载」职责，系统就会长期处在双份状态，难以判断哪一份才是应该被共享、备份和恢复的权威版本。
+
+将共享职责收敛到 `memorytree` 分支后，语义会明显更稳定。
+
+### 3.3 简化产品心智模型
+
+现有的 `5m heartbeat + 30m refresh` 设计会让用户反复追问：
+
+- 哪个动作在 `5m` 做
+- 哪个动作在 `30m` 做
+- 为什么主目录看起来仍然像 `5m` 在更新
+
+如果主目录只是缓存镜像，那么产品可以统一解释为：
+
+- heartbeat 固定周期运行
+- 产出写入 `memorytree` 分支
+- 开发目录只是同步后的本地镜像
+
+### 3.4 让同事更容易理解职责边界
+
+对于团队协作来说，更清晰的边界是：
+
+- 要共享和传播，去看 `memorytree` 分支
+- 要本地查看和继续工作，直接看当前开发目录的缓存镜像
+- 不再要求普通开发分支天然承载所有记忆文件
+
+## 4. 范围边界
+
+本方案默认采用下面的边界：
+
+- `AGENTS.md`、`Memory/01-07` 都属于 MemoryTree 内容
+- 这些内容都应进入 `memorytree` 分支
+- 开发目录中的对应文件只作为缓存镜像存在
+- 不要求重写旧 Git 历史
+- 不要求一开始就做复杂迁移兼容
+
+## 5. 第一阶段：必须完成
+
+### 1. 冻结新的产品规则
+
+目的：
+
+- 统一团队口径，避免后续实现过程中再次摇摆
+
+本项要做的事：
+
+- 明确写死 `memorytree` 分支是唯一共享记忆源
+- 明确写死开发目录中的 `AGENTS.md` 和 `Memory/**` 只是缓存镜像
+- 明确写死主开发分支不再承担记忆传播职责
+
+完成标准：
+
+- README、设计文档、规则文档不再出现双份承载的模糊表述
+
+### 2. 去掉 `30m refresh` 的对外产品语义
+
+目的：
+
+- 降低模型复杂度
+- 消除当前实现与说明之间的理解偏差
+
+本项要做的事：
+
+- 不再把 `refresh_interval = 30m` 作为推荐默认行为对外描述
+- Quick Start、CLI help、README 示例不再强调 `5m heartbeat + 30m refresh`
+- 把主目录缓存同步解释为 heartbeat 工作流的一部分，而不是独立产品能力
+
+完成标准：
+
+- 用户不再需要理解一个额外的 `30m refresh` 概念
+
+### 3. 补齐 `memorytree` 分支的提交闭环
+
+目的：
+
+- 让 `memorytree` 分支真正承载全部共享记忆，而不是只承载其中一部分
+
+本项要做的事：
+
+- 让 `AGENTS.md` 进入自动提交范围
+- 让 `Memory/01-05` 的同步结果在需要时进入自动提交范围
+- 重新确认 `Memory/06` 的 clean、manifest、raw、full 等子路径如何统一进入 `memorytree` 分支
+- 不再让「某些文件同步过去了，但没有被自动 commit」成为常态
+
+完成标准：
+
+- memory worktree 中形成的完整 MemoryTree 内容都能稳定进入 `memorytree` 分支
+
+### 4. 调整 heartbeat 的执行顺序
+
+目的：
+
+- 避免 `memorytree` 分支只保存半成品状态
+
+本项要做的事：
+
+- 先同步开发目录缓存到 worktree
+- 再导入 transcript
+- 再生成 report
+- 最后统一 commit/push
+
+完成标准：
+
+- 一轮 heartbeat 结束后，远程 `memorytree` 分支就是该轮完整产出的快照
+
+### 5. 对开发目录执行一次性退跟踪切换
+
+目的：
+
+- 让开发目录中的缓存镜像从下一版本开始默认不参与正常 Git 提交流程
+
+本项要做的事：
+
+- 在 `.gitignore` 中加入 `AGENTS.md` 和 `Memory/**` 的规则
+- 将当前已跟踪的 `AGENTS.md` 和 `Memory/**` 从 Git index 中移除
+- 保留本地文件，不删除开发目录缓存内容
+- 验证 heartbeat 仍然能在开发目录写入和同步这些缓存文件
+
+完成标准：
+
+- 开发目录中的缓存镜像继续存在，但默认不会进入常规开发提交
+
+### 6. 统一 README、设计文档和规则文档
+
+目的：
+
+- 防止实现改了、说明还停留在旧模型
+
+本项要做的事：
+
+- 更新 README 的产品定位、架构说明、Heartbeat 说明、配置说明
+- 更新 worktree 设计文档中的同步语义和缓存语义
+- 更新 Git policy、update rules、transcript archive 等规则文档
+- 清除「开发目录里的 Memory 文件默认可提交」这类旧表述
+
+完成标准：
+
+- 同事只看文档就能理解新的单一真源模型
+
+## 6. 第二阶段：后续收口
+
+### 7. 清理 `refresh_interval` 的配置和 CLI 残留
+
+目的：
+
+- 把第一阶段的产品语义调整真正落实到配置模型和命令界面
+
+本项要做的事：
+
+- 评估是否彻底移除 `refresh_interval`
+- 如果短期保留兼容字段，也要去掉它的主导地位

+
+完成标准：
+
+- 对外界面不再把 `refresh_interval` 当成核心能力
+
+### 8. 重写 `init` / `upgrade` / onboarding 文案
+
+目的：
+
+- 让新用户从第一次接触起就理解「memorytree 分支是真源，开发目录是缓存」
+
+本项要做的事：
+
+- 调整初始化与升级提示
+- 调整 Quick Start 的解释方式
+- 调整技能文档和中文说明文档
+
+完成标准：
+
+- 新用户不会再把主目录误认为正式共享记忆层
+
+### 9. 做跨平台与 E2E 回归验证
+
+目的：
+
+- 保证新模型在 Windows、macOS、Linux 上都能稳定工作
+
+本项要做的事：
+
+- 验证 worktree 同步、退跟踪后的主目录状态、heartbeat 提交闭环
+- 验证 report、recall、discover、daemon quick-start 等关键路径
+- 覆盖 main directory 缓存镜像持续写入的场景
+
+完成标准：
+
+- 新的单一真源模型有跨平台自动化验证支撑
+
+### 10. 评估是否处理旧 Git 历史中的敏感内容
+
+目的：
+
+- 明确当前版本切换是否只面向未来，还是还要额外清理历史
+
+本项要做的事：
+
+- 判断是否需要对历史中的 `AGENTS.md` / `Memory/**` 做额外风险评估
+- 判断是否需要清洗旧历史或只从下一版本开始执行新规则
+
+完成标准：
+
+- 团队对历史风险边界有明确结论，不再含糊
+
+## 7. 实施顺序建议
+
+建议按下面顺序推进：
+
+1. 先冻结产品规则
+2. 再补齐 `memorytree` 分支提交闭环
+3. 再调整 heartbeat 执行顺序
+4. 再做开发目录退跟踪切换
+5. 最后统一文档和规则文件

+

+
+## 8. 非目标
+
+当前方案不把下面这些事作为第一阶段前提：

+- 不依赖定时 branch merge
+- 不让开发目录重新承担共享记忆职责
+
+## 9. 同步给同事时的简版说明

*** Add File: d:\demo1\memorytree-workflow\docs\记忆分支单一真源两阶段改造方案.md
+# 记忆分支单一真源两阶段改造方案
+
+## 1. 背景
+
+当前 MemoryTree 的实现采用了「开发目录 + memory worktree + 全局归档」三层结构。
+
+它已经具备 worktree 隔离和 `memorytree` 分支自动提交能力，但产品语义仍然保留了两套承载方式：
+
+- `memorytree` 分支承担一部分共享记忆
+- 开发目录中的 `AGENTS.md` 和 `Memory/**` 仍被视为可见且可提交的仓库内容
+
+这会带来三个直接问题：
+
+- 共享记忆的唯一来源不清晰，容易形成双份承载
+- 开发目录容易出现敏感内容误提交
+- `5m heartbeat + 30m refresh` 的模型增加理解和维护成本
+
+## 2. 新目标
+
+本次改造要把产品模型统一为：
+
+- `memorytree` 分支和对应的 memory worktree 是唯一共享记忆源
+- 开发目录中的 `AGENTS.md` 和 `Memory/**` 全部都是本地缓存镜像
+- 开发目录缓存允许被 heartbeat 持续同步和覆盖，但默认不参与常规 Git 提交
+- heartbeat 的外部语义统一为固定短周期执行，不再对外强调单独的 `30m refresh` 模型
+- 所有 MemoryTree 内容最终都应完整进入 `memorytree` 分支，而不是只进入其中一部分
+
+## 3. 为什么要改
+
+### 3.1 降低误提交和敏感信息暴露风险
+
+当前主目录中的 `Memory/**` 很容易出现在 `git status` 中。只要用户习惯性提交，就可能把 transcript、报告和其他记忆内容一起推到正常开发分支。
+
+把开发目录改成缓存层之后，常规开发提交流程就不再默认携带这些内容。
+
+### 3.2 建立单一真源
+
+只要主目录和 `memorytree` 分支都在承担「正式记忆承载」职责，系统就会长期处在双份状态，难以判断哪一份才是应该被共享、备份和恢复的权威版本。
+
+将共享职责收敛到 `memorytree` 分支后，语义会明显更稳定。
+
+### 3.3 简化产品心智模型
+
+现有的 `5m heartbeat + 30m refresh` 设计会让用户反复追问：
+
+- 哪个动作在 `5m` 做
+- 哪个动作在 `30m` 做
+- 为什么主目录看起来仍然像 `5m` 在更新
+
+如果主目录只是缓存镜像，那么产品可以统一解释为：
+
+- heartbeat 固定周期运行
+- 产出写入 `memorytree` 分支
+- 开发目录只是同步后的本地镜像
+
+### 3.4 让同事更容易理解职责边界
+
+对于团队协作来说，更清晰的边界是：
+
+- 要共享和传播，去看 `memorytree` 分支
+- 要本地查看和继续工作，直接看当前开发目录的缓存镜像
+- 不再要求普通开发分支天然承载所有记忆文件
+
+## 4. 范围边界
+
+本方案默认采用下面的边界：
+
+- `AGENTS.md`、`Memory/01-07` 都属于 MemoryTree 内容
+- 这些内容都应进入 `memorytree` 分支
+- 开发目录中的对应文件只作为缓存镜像存在
+- 不要求重写旧 Git 历史
+- 不要求一开始就做复杂迁移兼容
+
+## 5. 第一阶段：必须完成
+
+### 1. 冻结新的产品规则
+
+目的：
+
+- 统一团队口径，避免后续实现过程中再次摇摆
+
+本项要做的事：
+
+- 明确写死 `memorytree` 分支是唯一共享记忆源
+- 明确写死开发目录中的 `AGENTS.md` 和 `Memory/**` 只是缓存镜像
+- 明确写死主开发分支不再承担记忆传播职责
+
+完成标准：
+
+- README、设计文档、规则文档不再出现双份承载的模糊表述
+
+### 2. 去掉 `30m refresh` 的对外产品语义
+
+目的：
+
+- 降低模型复杂度
+- 消除当前实现与说明之间的理解偏差
+
+本项要做的事：
+
+- 不再把 `refresh_interval = 30m` 作为推荐默认行为对外描述

+完成标准：

+完成标准：

+### 4. 调整 heartbeat 的执行顺序

+本项要做的事：

+- 再导入 transcript

+- 最后统一 commit/push

+### 5. 对开发目录执行一次性退跟踪切换

+

# Project Todo

## Binding
- Goal version: goal_v001
- Todo version: v001_001
- Created: 2026-03-19
- Status: active

## Milestones
- [x] Integrate `report_port` config handling and land the change on local `ts` and `main`.
- [x] Register the current repository with heartbeat while preserving the existing `openmnemo` registration.
- [x] Stand up local report serving on port `10010` and automate GitHub Pages publishing for this repository.

## In Progress
- Review and clear pending heartbeat alerts, especially `openmnemo` push failures and sensitive-match warnings.
- Align the next MemoryTree architecture change around a single shared-memory source: keep `memorytree` as the only Git-backed memory branch, turn development-directory `AGENTS.md` and `Memory/**` into local cache mirrors, remove the public `30m refresh` model, and stage the work through a documented two-phase plan before implementation.

## Done
- Repaired the current repository's local report access path after a user-facing outage: fixed the `memorytree caddy status` false negative by sending an allowed `Origin` header to the Caddy admin API, corrected the `memorytree-workflow` project entry back to `report_exposure = "lan"` in `~/.memorytree/config.toml`, re-enabled managed Caddy on port `10010`, and re-verified `http://127.0.0.1:10010/`, `http://localhost:10010/`, and `http://192.168.1.99:10010/` all return `200` while `caddy status` now reports `running: yes` and `using managed config: yes`.
- Removed a frontend timing bug from the generated report shell by deferring sidebar-collapse body access until `DOMContentLoaded`, rebuilt `Memory/07_reports`, and confirmed the dashboard loads in-browser without the prior `document.body.classList` error.
- Synced the local Codex `memorytree-workflow` skill wording to the current heartbeat model so it now treats `daemon quick-start` as the recommended current-repository setup path, keeps `daemon register` as the advanced customization path, and reserves `daemon install` for machine-level scheduler restore.
- Reinstalled the machine-level heartbeat scheduler on 2026-03-22 at a `5m` interval with `auto_push=true`, then verified the natural scheduled run at 17:07 completed successfully with `Last Result = 0` and a full `memorytree-workflow` import/push/report/sync cycle in the heartbeat log.
- User explicitly approved committing and pushing repo-local raw transcript mirrors for this repository on 2026-03-21, so `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` may now be included in dedicated MemoryTree-only Git branches and PRs.
- Fixed the remaining onboarding review gaps so `memorytree upgrade --format json` now prints the heartbeat quick-start hint to stderr without polluting JSON stdout, README now shows a truly custom advanced `daemon register` example instead of `--quick-start`, and the follow-up was re-verified with `npm test -- tests/cli/init-upgrade.test.ts`, `npm run typecheck`, `npm run build`, and `npm run test:e2e -- --run tests/e2e/cli-workflow.test.ts`.
- Clarified onboarding semantics so `init` and `upgrade` stay repository-only scaffold steps, `daemon quick-start` is the explicit quick-install path into heartbeat, and `daemon register` is framed as advanced setup; updated CLI help, next-step prompts, README wording, and tests to match.
- Merged the report hub so `Memory/07_reports/projects/index.html` now combines manifests from all registered projects (`memorytree-workflow`, `openmnemo`, and `papaweb`), and committed the change on `main` as `e062caa`.
- Verified local report access on `http://127.0.0.1:10010/`.
- Verified LAN access on `http://192.168.1.99:10010/`.
- Published the report to `gh-pages` and confirmed GitHub Pages at `https://beyondchenlin.github.io/memorytree-workflow/`.
- Fixed the scheduled publisher task and verified successful runs with `LastTaskResult = 0`.
- Drafted a worktree-based heartbeat upgrade document for isolating MemoryTree automation from the main development directory.
- Incorporated review decisions into the worktree upgrade document, including project-level config, a single global scheduler, and visible/committable transcript copies in the development directory.
- Implemented the project-level heartbeat config model with per-project heartbeat, refresh, report, and timestamp fields while keeping old TOML compatible.
- Implemented dedicated worktree bootstrap plus directional sync between development directories and MemoryTree worktrees.
- Added `memorytree daemon register` and targeted `memorytree daemon run-once --root <repo> --force` support for worktree-backed activation and manual execution.
- Verified the new worktree flow with unit tests, full unit suite, lint, typecheck, and CLI e2e coverage.
- Refined registration so Quick Start defaults the MemoryTree branch to `memorytree`, detailed settings can override it per project, and the first upstream binding is created automatically when `auto_push` is enabled.
- Added `memorytree daemon quick-start --root <repo>` as a one-command setup path that installs the scheduler when needed, registers the current repository with recommended defaults, and runs one immediate heartbeat sync.
- Added scenario-based `--help` guidance for `memorytree daemon`, `daemon install`, `daemon register`, `daemon run-once`, and `daemon quick-start`, then updated README and Chinese docs to recommend the new entry point.
- Verified the daemon simplification changes with `npm run typecheck`, `npm run lint`, `npm test`, and `npm run test:e2e`.
- Fixed two quick-start follow-up regressions: rerunning registration now preserves an existing repository's custom MemoryTree settings, and `daemon quick-start` now checks the installed scheduler cadence before recalibrating an already-installed but too-slow machine scheduler.
- Reframed local report access so Caddy is the primary long-running host, `report serve` is the temporary fallback path, and updated CLI help plus docs to match.
- Drafted a first-stage Caddy auto-management design document covering project-scoped Caddy config fragments, proposed `caddy enable|disable|status` commands, and conflict handling.
- Refined the Caddy auto-management design after review by locking it to a MemoryTree-owned main Caddyfile, adding a project-level report exposure rule with `local` as the safe default, and switching fragment naming to use stable `project.id` values.
- Implemented first-stage project-managed Caddy support with `memorytree caddy enable|disable|status`, a MemoryTree-owned `~/.memorytree/caddy/` config tree, project fragment filenames based on stable `project.id`, and local-only-by-default exposure via `report_exposure`.
- Added config parsing and roundtrip support for global and project-level `report_exposure`, plus unit coverage for config resolution, Caddy fragment rendering, command behavior, and CLI help output.
- Verified the Caddy phase-1 implementation with `npm run typecheck`, `npm run lint`, `npm test`, `npm run test:e2e -- --run tests/e2e/cli-workflow.test.ts`, and a real `caddy adapt` validation for both `local` and `lan` generated site blocks.
- Committed the Caddy phase-1 implementation as `f47643a` on `main` and pushed it to `origin/main`.
- Enabled the current repository in MemoryTree-managed Caddy and verified local HTTP access on `http://127.0.0.1:10010/` and `http://localhost:10010/`.
- Refreshed `README.md` after a full code walkthrough so it now reflects the current worktree-backed heartbeat architecture, managed Caddy flow, recommended `daemon quick-start` setup, configuration model, explicit `doubao` import path, and current validation coverage.
- Verified the current repository state after the README refresh with `npm run typecheck`, `npm run lint`, `npm run build`, `npm test`, and `npm run test:e2e`, then applied a small README header polish before preparing the docs update for GitHub.
- Committed the README refresh as `7269dc3` with title `docs(readme): refresh workflow overview` and pushed it to `origin/main` after the full validation stack passed.
- Expanded the GitHub Actions E2E workflow from Ubuntu-only to a three-platform matrix on Linux, macOS, and Windows, updated README validation wording to match, and re-verified the local Windows-side E2E suite with `npm run test:e2e`.
- Diagnosed the first PR failures as two separate cross-platform issues: a Windows-only absolute-path assertion in the Caddy manager test, and worktree rerun logic that mis-compared equivalent repository paths on macOS (`/var` vs `/private/var`) and Windows (short vs long path forms); fixed both and re-verified with `npm run typecheck`, `npm run lint`, `npm test`, and `npm run test:e2e`.
- Repaired the current repository's heartbeat branch conflict by renaming the stale `memorytree/memorytree-workflow` worktree branch to a dated backup branch, registering a fresh dedicated worktree on branch `memorytree` at `C:/Users/ai/.memorytree/worktrees/memorytree-workflow-active`, cleaning the duplicate Windows-case project entry in `~/.memorytree/config.toml`, and verifying `node dist/cli.js daemon run-once --root D:/demo1/memorytree-workflow --force` completes with transcript import, commit, push, report generation, and sync-back to the development directory.
- Drafted a Chinese two-phase planning document for the next MemoryTree model change so teammates can review one clear proposal for making `memorytree` the sole shared-memory source, treating the development directory as a cache mirror, removing the public `30m refresh` model, and sequencing the required implementation and follow-up work.

## Pending
- Review and approve the two-phase single-source memory plan before implementation.
- After approval, implement phase 1 first: close the `memorytree` branch commit gap, reorder heartbeat so reports land in the same commit cycle, switch the development directory to ignored cache mirrors, and then update the public docs and policy references.
- After phase 1 is stable, implement phase 2 cleanup: retire or demote `refresh_interval` in the CLI/config surface, refresh onboarding wording, and re-run cross-platform validation.

- Todo version: v001_001
- Created: 2026-03-19
- Status: active

## Milestones
- [x] Integrate `report_port` config handling and land the change on local `ts` and `main`.
- [x] Register the current repository with heartbeat while preserving the existing `openmnemo` registration.
- [x] Stand up local report serving on port `10010` and automate GitHub Pages publishing for this repository.

## In Progress
- Review and clear pending heartbeat alerts, especially `openmnemo` push failures and sensitive-match warnings.
- Align the next MemoryTree architecture change around a single shared-memory source: keep `memorytree` as the only Git-backed memory branch, turn development-directory `AGENTS.md` and `Memory/**` into local cache mirrors, remove the public `30m refresh` model, and stage the work through a documented two-phase plan before implementation.

## Done
- Repaired the current repository's local report access path after a user-facing outage: fixed the `memorytree caddy status` false negative by sending an allowed `Origin` header to the Caddy admin API, corrected the `memorytree-workflow` project entry back to `report_exposure = "lan"` in `~/.memorytree/config.toml`, re-enabled managed Caddy on port `10010`, and re-verified `http://127.0.0.1:10010/`, `http://localhost:10010/`, and `http://192.168.1.99:10010/` all return `200` while `caddy status` now reports `running: yes` and `using managed config: yes`.
- Removed a frontend timing bug from the generated report shell by deferring sidebar-collapse body access until `DOMContentLoaded`, rebuilt `Memory/07_reports`, and confirmed the dashboard loads in-browser without the prior `document.body.classList` error.
- Synced the local Codex `memorytree-workflow` skill wording to the current heartbeat model so it now treats `daemon quick-start` as the recommended current-repository setup path, keeps `daemon register` as the advanced customization path, and reserves `daemon install` for machine-level scheduler restore.
- Reinstalled the machine-level heartbeat scheduler on 2026-03-22 at a `5m` interval with `auto_push=true`, then verified the natural scheduled run at 17:07 completed successfully with `Last Result = 0` and a full `memorytree-workflow` import/push/report/sync cycle in the heartbeat log.
- User explicitly approved committing and pushing repo-local raw transcript mirrors for this repository on 2026-03-21, so `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` may now be included in dedicated MemoryTree-only Git branches and PRs.
- Fixed the remaining onboarding review gaps so `memorytree upgrade --format json` now prints the heartbeat quick-start hint to stderr without polluting JSON stdout, README now shows a truly custom advanced `daemon register` example instead of `--quick-start`, and the follow-up was re-verified with `npm test -- tests/cli/init-upgrade.test.ts`, `npm run typecheck`, `npm run build`, and `npm run test:e2e -- --run tests/e2e/cli-workflow.test.ts`.
- Clarified onboarding semantics so `init` and `upgrade` stay repository-only scaffold steps, `daemon quick-start` is the explicit quick-install path into heartbeat, and `daemon register` is framed as advanced setup; updated CLI help, next-step prompts, README wording, and tests to match.
- Merged the report hub so `Memory/07_reports/projects/index.html` now combines manifests from all registered projects (`memorytree-workflow`, `openmnemo`, and `papaweb`), and committed the change on `main` as `e062caa`.
- Verified local report access on `http://127.0.0.1:10010/`.
- Verified LAN access on `http://192.168.1.99:10010/`.
- Published the report to `gh-pages` and confirmed GitHub Pages at `https://beyondchenlin.github.io/memorytree-workflow/`.
- Fixed the scheduled publisher task and verified successful runs with `LastTaskResult = 0`.
- Drafted a worktree-based heartbeat upgrade document for isolating MemoryTree automation from the main development directory.
- Incorporated review decisions into the worktree upgrade document, including project-level config, a single global scheduler, and visible/committable transcript copies in the development directory.
- Implemented the project-level heartbeat config model with per-project heartbeat, refresh, report, and timestamp fields while keeping old TOML compatible.
- Implemented dedicated worktree bootstrap plus directional sync between development directories and MemoryTree worktrees.
- Added `memorytree daemon register` and targeted `memorytree daemon run-once --root <repo> --force` support for worktree-backed activation and manual execution.
- Verified the new worktree flow with unit tests, full unit suite, lint, typecheck, and CLI e2e coverage.
- Refined registration so Quick Start defaults the MemoryTree branch to `memorytree`, detailed settings can override it per project, and the first upstream binding is created automatically when `auto_push` is enabled.
- Added `memorytree daemon quick-start --root <repo>` as a one-command setup path that installs the scheduler when needed, registers the current repository with recommended defaults, and runs one immediate heartbeat sync.
- Added scenario-based `--help` guidance for `memorytree daemon`, `daemon install`, `daemon register`, `daemon run-once`, and `daemon quick-start`, then updated README and Chinese docs to recommend the new entry point.
- Verified the daemon simplification changes with `npm run typecheck`, `npm run lint`, `npm test`, and `npm run test:e2e`.
- Fixed two quick-start follow-up regressions: rerunning registration now preserves an existing repository's custom MemoryTree settings, and `daemon quick-start` now checks the installed scheduler cadence before recalibrating an already-installed but too-slow machine scheduler.
- Reframed local report access so Caddy is the primary long-running host, `report serve` is the temporary fallback path, and updated CLI help plus docs to match.
- Drafted a first-stage Caddy auto-management design document covering project-scoped Caddy config fragments, proposed `caddy enable|disable|status` commands, and conflict handling.
- Refined the Caddy auto-management design after review by locking it to a MemoryTree-owned main Caddyfile, adding a project-level report exposure rule with `local` as the safe default, and switching fragment naming to use stable `project.id` values.
- Implemented first-stage project-managed Caddy support with `memorytree caddy enable|disable|status`, a MemoryTree-owned `~/.memorytree/caddy/` config tree, project fragment filenames based on stable `project.id`, and local-only-by-default exposure via `report_exposure`.
- Added config parsing and roundtrip support for global and project-level `report_exposure`, plus unit coverage for config resolution, Caddy fragment rendering, command behavior, and CLI help output.
- Verified the Caddy phase-1 implementation with `npm run typecheck`, `npm run lint`, `npm test`, `npm run test:e2e -- --run tests/e2e/cli-workflow.test.ts`, and a real `caddy adapt` validation for both `local` and `lan` generated site blocks.
- Committed the Caddy phase-1 implementation as `f47643a` on `main` and pushed it to `origin/main`.
- Enabled the current repository in MemoryTree-managed Caddy and verified local HTTP access on `http://127.0.0.1:10010/` and `http://localhost:10010/`.
- Refreshed `README.md` after a full code walkthrough so it now reflects the current worktree-backed heartbeat architecture, managed Caddy flow, recommended `daemon quick-start` setup, configuration model, explicit `doubao` import path, and current validation coverage.
- Verified the current repository state after the README refresh with `npm run typecheck`, `npm run lint`, `npm run build`, `npm test`, and `npm run test:e2e`, then applied a small README header polish before preparing the docs update for GitHub.
- Committed the README refresh as `7269dc3` with title `docs(readme): refresh workflow overview` and pushed it to `origin/main` after the full validation stack passed.
- Expanded the GitHub Actions E2E workflow from Ubuntu-only to a three-platform matrix on Linux, macOS, and Windows, updated README validation wording to match, and re-verified the local Windows-side E2E suite with `npm run test:e2e`.
- Diagnosed the first PR failures as two separate cross-platform issues: a Windows-only absolute-path assertion in the Caddy manager test, and worktree rerun logic that mis-compared equivalent repository paths on macOS (`/var` vs `/private/var`) and Windows (short vs long path forms); fixed both and re-verified with `npm run typecheck`, `npm run lint`, `npm test`, and `npm run test:e2e`.
- Repaired the current repository's heartbeat branch conflict by renaming the stale `memorytree/memorytree-workflow` worktree branch to a dated backup branch, registering a fresh dedicated worktree on branch `memorytree` at `C:/Users/ai/.memorytree/worktrees/memorytree-workflow-active`, cleaning the duplicate Windows-case project entry in `~/.memorytree/config.toml`, and verifying `node dist/cli.js daemon run-once --root D:/demo1/memorytree-workflow --force` completes with transcript import, commit, push, report generation, and sync-back to the development directory.
- Drafted a Chinese two-phase planning document for the next MemoryTree model change so teammates can review one clear proposal for making `memorytree` the sole shared-memory source, treating the development directory as a cache mirror, removing the public `30m refresh` model, and sequencing the required implementation and follow-up work.

## Pending
- Review and approve the two-phase single-source memory plan before implementation.
- After approval, implement phase 1 first: close the `memorytree` branch commit gap, reorder heartbeat so reports land in the same commit cycle, switch the development directory to ignored cache mirrors, and then update the public docs and policy references.
- After phase 1 is stable, implement phase 2 cleanup: retire or demote `refresh_interval` in the CLI/config surface, refresh onboarding wording, and re-run cross-platform validation.
